The year turns

Year of the Fire Horse

2026. Once in sixty years the Horse catches fire, a year that rewards the bold and scorches the hesitant. If the Horse is yours, this is your decade's hinge.

諺語 · chinese proverbs

The Proverb Pond

Eighty-seven Chinese proverbs, carried down from the 道德經, the Analects, the histories, and the mouths of ordinary people. Each keeps its sound, its meaning, and a reading of its own. Draw from the water to let it choose a pond for you, or pick one and read the whole library below.

Reach into the watertouch the pond, and a proverb rises to meet you

Draw from the pond to let the water choose a current, or pick one.

The Way of Water23 proverbs · the art of not forcingEnter the pondTiming & Fortune's Turning40 proverbs · luck turns, and turns againEnter the pondPerseverance & the Long Road34 proverbs · the long, unglamorous middleEnter the pondCourage & Decisive Action38 proverbs · the moment you cannot rehearseEnter the pondHome, Family & Roots30 proverbs · where you are from, and forEnter the pondWisdom & Learning82 proverbs · knowing, and knowing you do notEnter the pondHarmony, Virtue & Balance66 proverbs · the weight a steady character carriesEnter the pondWealth, Work & Diligence36 proverbs · money, patience, the wealth cornerEnter the pondFriendship, Trust & Speech40 proverbs · who stays, and what you sayEnter the pondHumility & Self-Mastery52 proverbs · the quiet that outlasts the loudEnter the pondAdversity & Resilience28 proverbs · what the cold shows about the pineEnter the pondNature, Seasons & Health31 proverbs · the body kept in seasonEnter the pond

The whole library

Every one of the 500 proverbs, each with its sound, its meaning, a reading, and its source. Filter by element, pond, zodiac, type, or focus, or search.

Element
Theme
Zodiac
Type
Focus
01

上shàng善shàn若ruò水shuǐ

shàng shàn ruò shuǐ

the highest good is like water

The finest virtue is like water, which benefits all things and flows to the low places without contending.

Water asks for nothing and shapes everything. It sinks to the low room others avoid and turns even that into a home. To be strong like water is to stop contending and still reach everywhere.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 8 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
02

天tiān下xià之zhī至zhì柔róu，馳chí騁chěng天tiān下xià之zhī至zhì堅jiān

tiān xià zhī zhì róu chí chěng tiān xià zhī zhì jiān

the softest thing under heaven gallops over the hardest thing under heaven

The most yielding force in the world overcomes the most unyielding; water wears through stone.

Water has no pride to protect, no position to defend. It goes where it is sent and moves by the shape of what it finds. That is how it ends up everywhere — and why everything else ends up shaped by it.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 43 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
03

心xīn如rú止zhǐ水shuǐ

xīn rú zhǐ shuǐ

heart like still water

A heart as still and clear as motionless water; profound inner calm.

The still water shows the sky perfectly because it has stopped insisting on its own motion. The heart that has reached this quality of calm does not reflect events back at itself but receives them clearly, as they are, undistorted by the turbulence of what the heart is still working out about the world. Still water is the condition for seeing true.

成語 Adage The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Zhuangzi 莊子·德充符 (Dé Chōng Fú, Fullness of Virtue)

Read the full reading
04

無wú為wéi不bù爭zhēng，而ér天tiān下xià莫mò能néng與yǔ

wú wéi bù zhēng ér tiān xià mò néng yǔ

non-doing non-contending, and none under heaven can rival

By not contending, one cannot be overcome; the power of non-competition.

Water, which contends with nothing and refuses no low place, wears down the stone that force cannot move. The person who does not scramble for position finds that positions come to them, because they are the only one who is not competing for everything and can therefore be trusted with it. Non-striving is not passivity but a different kind of power entirely.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第六十六章 (Chapter 66)

Read the full reading
05

無wú為wéi而ér無wú不bù為wéi

wú wéi ér wú bù wéi

through non-action, nothing is left undone

By working with the natural flow of things rather than forcing them, everything finds its own resolution.

The stream does not try to reach the sea. It simply flows where the landscape sends it, and eventually arrives. The person who acts this way — not inert, but aligned — gets more done than the one who strains against every current. Less force, more arrival.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 48 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
06

致zhì虛xū守shǒu靜jìng

zhì xū shǒu jìng

attain emptiness, hold to stillness

Cultivate inner emptiness and hold fast to stillness; from that quiet the ten thousand things return.

A bowl is useful because of what it does not contain. A room breathes because of the space between its walls. The person who clears themselves of noise becomes the one everyone wants to sit near.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 16 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
07

順shùn水shuǐ推tuī舟zhōu

shùn shuǐ tuī zhōu

follow the current to push the boat

Go with the natural flow of events; act in accord with the situation.

The current already knows where it is going. The boatman who fights it spends everything to arrive exhausted; the one who reads it and leans slightly arrives with energy to spare. There is a grace in not confusing resistance with strength.

成語 Adage The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Ming Dynasty 明·馮夢龍《醒世恆言》 (Xǐng Shì Héng Yán)

Read the full reading
08

天tiān下xià本běn無wú事shì

tiān xià běn wú shì

under heaven there is originally nothing

Most troubles are created by people who stir them up; peace is the natural state.

The still pond does not become turbulent by itself. Most of what disrupts the natural peace is introduced by a human hand, usually out of ambition, fear, or mischief. The natural condition of things, left alone, tends toward a kind of order. This is not naivety but observation.

諺語 Proverb The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Tang Dynasty 唐·韓愈 (Hán Yù) derived; common philosophical saying

Read the full reading
09

柔róu能néng克kè剛gāng

róu néng kè gāng

softness can overcome hardness

Flexibility and gentleness will outlast force and rigidity.

The willow bends in the storm that snaps the oak. Not because the willow is weak, but because it knows something the oak refuses to learn. Strength that cannot yield is only waiting to break.

成語 Adage The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 78 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
10

無wú中zhōng生shēng有yǒu

wú zhōng shēng yǒu

from nothing produce something

Create something from nothing; fabricate; also, the generative power of emptiness.

The Taoists understood this as a cosmological truth: the world came from the womb of not-yet-having. The strategist understands it as technique: invent what does not exist and the opponent must respond to it. Emptiness is not absence but potential, the field before the crop.

成語 Adage The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第四十章 (Chapter 40)

Read the full reading
11

無wú為wéi而ér治zhì

wú wéi ér zhì

non-action yet governing

Govern through non-interference; effective leadership through minimal coercion.

The best leader is the one whose people complete their work and say: we did this ourselves. Non-action is not absence but restraint, the discipline of not filling every silence or solving every problem before it has a chance to solve itself. The water governs the stone by refusing to fight it.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第三章 (Chapter 3)

Read the full reading
12

萬wàn變biàn不bù離lí其qí宗zōng

wàn biàn bù lí qí zōng

ten thousand changes do not leave the root

No matter how much changes, the essence remains the same.

The river takes a thousand shapes around stones and bends yet is always the same river, returning to its source after the long rains. Beneath all transformation there is a principle that holds still. Find that and the changes no longer frighten you.

諺語 Proverb The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese proverb (common in philosophical and folk contexts)

Read the full reading
13

逝shì者zhě如rú斯sī夫fū，不bù捨shě晝zhòu夜yè

shì zhě rú sī fū bù shě zhòu yè

that which passes is like this, not stopping day or night

Time flows like a river, never stopping day or night; the constant passing of all things.

Confucius stood at the river's edge and watched the water moving without pause and said: it is like this. He did not say how we should feel about the passing; he only named it plainly. The naming itself was the teaching: what is here now will be past before the sentence finishes. This is not cause for grief but for presence.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·子罕 (Zǐ Hǎn IX)

Read the full reading
14

人rén往wǎng高gāo處chù走zǒu，水shuǐ往wǎng低dī處chù流liú

rén wǎng gāo chù zǒu shuǐ wǎng dī chù liú

people walk toward higher places, water flows toward lower places

People naturally aspire upward; each follows their own nature.

The water and the person are both following the logic of their own nature, and both are right to do so. Water, by seeking the low place, eventually reaches the sea; people, by seeking the high place, eventually arrive where their aspiration pointed. Neither is fighting their nature; both are completing it. Let each thing move the way it was made to move.

諺語 Proverb The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (suyu)

Read the full reading
15

以yǐ柔róu克kè剛gāng

yǐ róu kè gāng

with soft overcome hard

Overcome the hard and strong with the soft and yielding; flexibility defeats rigidity.

Willow survives the storm that breaks the oak; it bends completely and returns. The oak insisted on its own nature against the wind and paid for it. There is a form of strength that does not look like strength from outside: the yielding that knows how to return, the softness that outlasts the hard thing it appeared to surrender to.

成語 Adage The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第七十八章 (Chapter 78)

Read the full reading
16

天tiān下xià皆jiē知zhī美měi之zhī為wéi美měi，斯sī惡è已yǐ

tiān xià jiē zhī měi zhī wéi měi sī è yǐ

when all under heaven know beauty as beauty, ugliness already exists

When beauty is universally recognized as beauty, its opposite already exists; duality is inherent.

The naming of the beautiful creates the ugly by contrast; they were always together, waiting for the first word to bring the second into existence. All definition creates its opposite, and the person who insists on absolute categories finds them dissolving at the edges where each touches the other. Hold definitions lightly; the thing they name is larger than they are.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第二章 (Chapter 2)

Read the full reading
17

為wéi而ér不bù爭zhēng

wéi ér bù zhēng

act without contending

Do the work without competing for recognition; the greatest victory is one that was never a contest.

The tide reaches every shore without announcing its arrival. It does not compete with the cliff, yet the cliff is shaped by it. To act without striving for supremacy is not passivity — it is the most enduring kind of power.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 81 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
18

見jiàn山shān是shì山shān，見jiàn水shuǐ是shì水shuǐ

jiàn shān shì shān jiàn shuǐ shì shuǐ

see mountain is mountain, see water is water

True enlightenment returns to simple, direct perception; after all the analysis, what is there is simply what is there.

The first stage sees a mountain and calls it a mountain. The second stage sees through the mountain to all its meanings and contingencies and no longer sees just a mountain. The third stage, harder than both, sees the mountain again — simply, freshly, without the weight of all the knowing. This last mountain is the most real one.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 土 Earth 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Qingyuan Weixin 青原惟信 (Song Dynasty Chan Buddhist master)

Read the full reading
19

曲qū則zé全quán

qū zé quán

yield, then remain whole

By bending, one keeps oneself intact; the one who never yields breaks.

What bows survives the wind. What bows survives the season. What bows survives until the sun returns and lifts it back toward the same height — still whole, still rooted, still itself.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 22 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
20

莊zhuāng周zhōu夢mèng蝶dié

zhuāng zhōu mèng dié

Zhuangzi dreams butterfly

The dream of transformation and the blurring of self and other; questioning the nature of identity.

He dreamed he was a butterfly, free and fluttering, and when he woke he could not determine whether he was a man who had dreamed of being a butterfly or a butterfly now dreaming it was a man. This is not madness but philosophy at its most honest: identity is more permeable than we pretend, and the edges of self are genuinely uncertain. Float in that uncertainty; it is more alive than the solid version.

詩詞 Verse The Way of Water Present-minded 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Zhuangzi 莊子·齊物論 (Qí Wù Lùn, On the Equalization of Things)

Read the full reading
21

隨suí遇yù而ér安ān

suí yù ér ān

following encounter, find peace

Find contentment wherever you land; be at ease with circumstances as they are.

The person who needs everything to be arranged exactly right before they can feel at home will feel at home nowhere. The person who can find something worth settling into wherever they land — they are already home, wherever they are.

成語 Adage The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Qing dynasty idiom; Liu E 劉鶚, 老殘遊記 (The Travels of Lao Can)

Read the full reading
22

隨suí機jī應yìng變biàn

suí jī yìng biàn

follow opportunity, respond to change

Adapt to circumstances as they change; respond flexibly to situations.

The water takes the shape of every vessel without losing its nature as water. Adaptation is not surrender but a kind of intelligence that knows the difference between what is essential and what is merely a current form. Stay water; change shape.

成語 Adage The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese proverb (common in military and strategic texts)

Read the full reading
23

順shùn其qí自zì然rán

shùn qí zì rán

follow its self-so

Let nature take its course; work with a thing rather than force it.

A room has its own light and its own flow, and so do you. The art is not to force either into a shape, but to move with what is already there until the two agree.

俗語 Saying The Way of Water Present-minded 水 Water 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Daoist idiom (zìrán)

Read the full reading
24

冬dōng至zhì陽yáng生shēng春chūn又yòu來lái

dōng zhì yáng shēng chūn yòu lái

winter solstice yang born, spring again comes

At the darkest moment of winter, yang energy is reborn and spring begins its return.

The winter solstice is the year's longest night and also the night after which every night gets shorter. At the very depth of the dark, the yang turns. This is the nature of all cycles: the reversal is hidden inside the extreme, and what seems like the lowest point is actually the first moment of the climb. Trust the solstice.

詩詞 Verse Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese seasonal observation (tied to Chinese calendar and cosmology)

Read the full reading
25

夜yè長cháng夢mèng多duō

yè cháng mèng duō

night long, dreams many

Delay leads to complications; the longer you wait, the more problems arise.

The night extended for tactical reasons accumulates its own complications: dreams that were not planned, arrivals that change the arrangement, questions that could not have been anticipated when the decision to wait was made. Procrastination is not a pause but a period in which the world continues to evolve, usually not in the direction of what was being protected.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in strategic and folk contexts)

Read the full reading
26

小xiǎo洞dòng不bù補bǔ，大dà洞dòng受shòu苦kǔ

xiǎo dòng bù bǔ dà dòng shòu kǔ

small hole not patched, large hole suffer

Fix small problems before they become large ones; neglecting small issues leads to big suffering.

The small hole in the dam wants so little to fix it: a handful of earth and an afternoon. The large hole in the dam is a different conversation entirely. The investment in the small is the prevention of the large, and what is prevented never shows its cost because it never happens. Prevention is the cheapest repair.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 土 Earth 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
27

福fú禍huò相xiāng依yī

fú huò xiāng yī

fortune and misfortune lean on each other

Good fortune and misfortune are intertwined; what seems bad may contain good.

The crack in the cup is also where the light enters. Every piece of luck carries the seed of its opposite, and every disaster turns the soil for what comes after. Those who can hold both possibilities at once move through the reversals without being broken by them.

詩詞 Verse Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第五十八章 (Chapter 58)

Read the full reading
28

水shuǐ到dào渠qú成chéng

shuǐ dào qú chéng

the water arrives, the channel forms

When conditions are ripe, results follow without forcing.

Stop digging the channel and tend the water instead. When the flow is full enough, the path it needs appears on its own, and what you wanted arrives without a fight.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 牛 Year of the Ox

Source recorded in Su Shi 蘇軾 (Song); folk saying

Read the full reading
29

滄cāng海hǎi桑sāng田tián

cāng hǎi sāng tián

the blue sea becomes mulberry fields

Vast, sweeping change over the long span of time.

The thing that feels permanent to you today is only permanent at the speed you are watching it. Where you stand was once seabed, and will be something you cannot picture. Hold your certainties gently, they are all mid-tide.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks back 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source 神仙傳

Read the full reading
30

當dāng機jī立lì斷duàn

dāng jī lì duàn

meeting the moment, decide at once

When the decisive moment arrives, act without hesitation.

You have rehearsed this choice a hundred times in the safety of your head. But the moment it truly arrives, thinking becomes a kind of flinching. There is an instant when the sword is already raised — cut then, cleanly, before doubt talks you back down the stairs.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Chen Lin 陳琳 (Three Kingdoms, Wei), 答東阿王牋 (originally 應機立斷); per Taiwan MOE 成語典

Read the full reading
31

養yǎng虎hǔ為wéi患huàn

yǎng hǔ wéi huàn

raise a tiger as a disaster

Nurturing a potential enemy or threat brings future trouble; leaving danger to grow.

The tiger fed by hand remembers the hand but does not become something other than a tiger. Tolerance extended without wisdom can feed what should have been contained, and what grows large in comfort does not shrink when comfort ends. The cub's charm is not a promise about the adult.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 木 Wood 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Shi Ji 史記·項羽本紀 (Xiàng Yǔ biography)

Read the full reading
32

福fú至zhì心xīn靈líng

fú zhì xīn líng

when good fortune arrives, the mind turns nimble

When luck comes, the mind sharpens and the right answers arrive with ease.

You have noticed it — how in a lucky season the words come out right, the ideas land, the timing is uncanny. It is not that you suddenly grew clever. Fortune, when it arrives, loosens something in you; the trick is to move while the door of the mind is swung wide.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 水 Water 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Bi Zhongxun 畢仲詢, 幕府燕閒錄 (Song dynasty); full form in Bai Pu 白樸, 東牆記 (Yuan)

Read the full reading
33

靜jìng待dài花huā開kāi

jìng dài huā kāi

quietly wait for the flower to open

Wait patiently for things to come to fruition in their own time.

The bud that is pried open before it is ready reveals a lesser version of what would have appeared on its own schedule, and the gardener's impatience has robbed the flower of its own timing. Some things ripen only when the conditions inside them are fully ready, and the most useful thing the waiting person can do is continue to water and trust the bud.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
34

厚hòu積jī薄bó發fā

hòu jī bó fā

accumulate deeply, release thinly

Accumulate extensively before expressing; build up over a long time and release at the right moment.

The deep reservoir releases with precision and control what the thin stream cannot: the stored force of years. What has been building in the underground takes no space that others can see or object to, and when it finally surfaces, the quality of its emergence reflects everything it was during the invisible years. Depth before breadth. Accumulation before expression.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Su Shi 蘇軾《稼說送張琥》 (Jià Shuō Sòng Zhāng Hǔ, Song Dynasty essay)

Read the full reading
35

喜xǐ從cóng天tiān降jiàng

xǐ cóng tiān jiàng

joy descends from heaven

Unexpected good news arrives; joy falls from the sky like a gift from heaven.

Some good news arrives not because it was built toward but because the sky of fortune opened at a particular moment and let something through that had nothing to do with calculation. These arrivals are not rewards but gifts, and the person who receives them without grasping can enjoy them completely. Open hands are the ones that catch what falls from heaven.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 火 Fire 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese festive expression (common at New Year and celebrations)

Read the full reading
36

天tiān時shí地dì利lì人rén和hé

tiān shí dì lì rén hé

heaven's timing, earth's advantage, human harmony

The three conditions of success align: the right timing, a favorable place, and united people.

Nothing great stands on timing alone, or place alone, or people alone. It stands where the three lean together, and place is the one you can arrange with your own hands.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Mencius 孟子, Gongsun Chou II

Read the full reading
37

天tiān羅luó地dì網wǎng

tiān luó dì wǎng

heaven net, earth net

A net spread across heaven and earth; an inescapable situation.

The net of consequences is woven wider than any single person can see from where they stand. What seemed like open space was threaded with invisible lines, and the moment of realization comes only when the net closes. This is why the wise consider carefully, and early.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 金 Metal 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Yuan Dynasty 元·施耐庵《水滸傳》 (Shuǐ Hǔ Zhuàn, Water Margin)

Read the full reading
38

山shān水shuǐ有yǒu相xiāng逢féng

shān shuǐ yǒu xiāng féng

mountain and water have their meeting

All rivers and roads meet again eventually; paths that diverge will cross again.

The mountain and the river do not choose each other; they simply coexist in the landscape, and the landscape brings them together at the valley. People who have shared a world once carry in them the topology of that world, and if they are still moving through the same broad landscape, the chances are reasonable that the paths will intersect again. Keep moving; the meeting will arrange itself.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
39

時shí來lái運yùn轉zhuǎn

shí lái yùn zhuǎn

when time comes, fortune turns

When the right time arrives, fortune changes; luck turns at the appointed moment.

The wheel of fortune is not indifferent; it has its own timing, and the person who has prepared well during the turn away will be positioned when the turn comes back. The waiting is not idle but the patient accumulation of what will be needed when the wheel faces forward again. The timing is not yours, but the readiness is.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk expression (common in theatrical and folk culture)

Read the full reading
40

時shí勢shì造zào英yīng雄xióng

shí shì zào yīng xióng

the tide of the times creates heroes

The moment and its circumstances are what raise ordinary people into extraordinary ones.

You imagine the great ones were simply born larger than you. Mostly they were people the hour reached for. When the tide turns your way — and it turns for everyone once — do not wait to feel worthy of it. The times do not summon the ready; they make the ready out of whoever steps forward.

俗語 Saying Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Attested in Liang Qichao 梁啟超, 李鴻章傳; also Bing Xin 冰心, 去國 — late Qing / early Republican formulation

Read the full reading
41

有yǒu緣yuán千qiān里lǐ來lái相xiāng會huì

yǒu yuán qiān lǐ lái xiāng huì

with fate, a thousand li come to meet

Those destined to meet will find each other across great distances.

The distance that separates two people who are meant to meet is not a barrier but simply the measure of what fate had to arrange in order for the meeting to happen in the right place at the right time. A thousand li of geography is a small objection for two people who share a page in the book of fated encounters.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common in love and destiny contexts)

Read the full reading
42

物wù極jí必bì反fǎn

wù jí bì fǎn

things reach extreme, must reverse

Anything taken to its extreme will reverse; excess always turns against itself.

The river that rises highest falls fastest. The voice that grows loudest is first to lose its audience. There is a ceiling woven into all things, and the wise hand pulls back just before touching it.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 金 Metal 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source 呂氏春秋; Daoist cyclical thought

Read the full reading
43

萬wàn事shì俱jù備bèi，只zhǐ欠qiàn東dōng風fēng

wàn shì jù bèi zhǐ qiàn dōng fēng

ten thousand things all prepared, only missing the east wind

Everything is ready except the one crucial element; almost but not quite complete.

Cao Cao's fleet was enormous and his preparations thorough and only the east wind was missing, and Zhuge Liang's greatest trick was borrowing it from the sky. Sometimes the final ingredient is the one that cannot be manufactured and must simply arrive. Prepare everything else so completely that the only thing left is the thing that comes when the time is right.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source San Guo Yan Yi 三國演義 (Sān Guó Yǎn Yì, Romance of the Three Kingdoms)

Read the full reading
44

長cháng風fēng破pò浪làng會huì有yǒu時shí

cháng fēng pò làng huì yǒu shí

strong wind breaking waves will have its time

The time will come to ride the wind and break through the waves; opportunity to succeed will arrive.

The wind that will carry you has not arrived yet, but the shore knows it is coming. Keep the sail ready; keep yourself ready. The moment when everything aligns will not announce itself in advance, and the vessel that is unready when the great wind rises simply watches it pass.

詩詞 Verse Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Li Bai 李白·《行路難》 (Xíng Lù Nán, The Hard Road, Tang Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
45

天tiān有yǒu不bù測cè風fēng雲yún，人rén有yǒu旦dàn夕xī禍huò福fú

tiān yǒu bù cè fēng yún rén yǒu dàn xī huò fú

the sky has unforeseen wind and cloud; people have morning-to-evening misfortune and fortune

Life is unpredictable; fortune and misfortune can arrive without warning, just as weather changes unexpectedly.

The sky that was blue at breakfast will sometimes bring rain by midday, and not a single cloud gave warning. This is not cruelty — it is simply the nature of what we are standing inside. The wise do not curse the rain. They keep a coat nearby.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Chinese folk proverb 民間諺語; common Qing dynasty usage

Read the full reading
46

事shì不bù可kě失shī，一yī事shì在zài人rén

shì bù kě shī yī shì zài rén

matters cannot be missed, one matter rests with person

No opportunity should be squandered; each affair ultimately depends on the person.

The clock of the opportunity does not stop to be consulted; it moves past while the consultation is happening. Each matter that passes through your hands carries some portion of its outcome in your direction, and the person who grasps this takes their portion without waiting to be reminded. Responsibility is not a burden but an invitation.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
47

塞sài翁wēng失shī馬mǎ

sài wēng shī mǎ

the frontier old man loses his horse

A blessing can wear the face of loss; fortune and misfortune cannot be judged in the moment.

You cannot read a morning by noon. The loss that empties you today may be the door you walk through next spring, so hold both grief and hope loosely.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 水 Water 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Huainanzi 淮南子, 2nd c. BCE

Read the full reading
48

天tiān高gāo皇huáng帝dì遠yuǎn

tiān gāo huáng dì yuǎn

the sky is high, the emperor is far away

Distant authority cannot control what happens at the local level; in remote places, people govern themselves.

There is a kind of freedom that only the far-away places know. Where no one is watching, things grow in their own shapes. The village beyond the mountain has its own understanding of what the rules mean — and often it is wiser than the center.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Chinese folk proverb 民間諺語; common from Song dynasty

Read the full reading
49

機jī不bù可kě失shī，時shí不bù再zài來lái

jī bù kě shī, shí bù zài lái

the chance must not be lost; the moment will not come again

Seize the opportunity now, for the same hour will never return.

There is a door that only stands open while you are looking at it. You will be tempted to wait until you are ready, until it is safe — but the moment does not wait to be convenient. Some things are offered exactly once, and the hesitation is the answer.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Old History of the Five Dynasties 舊五代史, 晉書·安重榮傳

Read the full reading
50

趁chèn熱rè打dǎ鐵tiě

chèn rè dǎ tiě

while hot strike the iron

Strike while the iron is hot; act at the right moment when conditions are favorable.

The iron holds its shape in the fire only so long, and the moment is brief. The smith who hesitates finds the metal hardened and the opportunity gone cold. Timing is not luck but attention made into action.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
51

遠yuǎn水shuǐ不bù救jiù近jìn火huǒ

yuǎn shuǐ bù jiù jìn huǒ

distant water cannot save near fire

A distant solution is no help for an immediate problem; use what is close and available.

The lake that cannot be reached in time is no lake at all to the burning house. The brilliant long-term strategy is irrelevant when the present urgency has its own timeline. Triage is wisdom: read the room, read the fire, and work with what is in arm's reach right now.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 水 Water 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Warring States 戰國策·韓策一 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Han Strategies I)

Read the full reading
52

馬mǎ到dào成chéng功gōng

mǎ dào chéng gōng

horse arrives, success achieved

Immediate success upon arrival; success from the moment of engagement.

The horse of action and the field of success are the same place at the same time; arrival and accomplishment collapse into one event when preparation is complete. The luck celebrated at the finish line was the readiness that preceded the starting signal. Success often looks sudden from outside.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Yuan Dynasty 元·關漢卿雜劇 (Guān Hàn Qīng dramatic works)

Read the full reading
53

吃chī虧kuī是shì福fú

chī kuī shì fú

to suffer a loss is fortune

Suffering loss now can be a blessing in disguise; what seems like a disadvantage may be good fortune.

The moment of loss is never when its value can be known. The position conceded, the argument not pressed, the pride that yielded — these things take years to reveal their returns. The person who has learned to hold the small loss lightly has also learned to recognize the larger gain it cleared the way for.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 土 Earth 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (common in folk wisdom)

Read the full reading
54

好hǎo事shì多duō磨mó

hǎo shì duō mó

good things suffer much grinding

Worthwhile outcomes rarely arrive without delay and obstacle.

The thing you want most is taking the longest, and you have started to read the delay as a no. Read it differently. What comes easily is rarely worth guarding; what is ground against stone this long is being made to last. The obstacle is not the detour — it is the toll.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 土 Earth 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Dong Jieyuan 董解元, 西廂記諸宮調 (Master Dong's Western Chamber Romance), Jin dynasty

Read the full reading
55

瓜guā熟shú蒂dì落luò

guā shú dì luò

when the melon ripens, the stalk falls

When conditions have matured, the result comes of its own accord.

You have been tugging at something that is not ready to come free, and the tugging is bruising it. A ripe thing lets go on its own, quietly, when you are looking elsewhere. Tend it, feed it, and stop pulling on the stem.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 土 Earth 羊 Year of the Goat

Source 雲笈七籤

Read the full reading
56

見jiàn機jī而ér作zuò，不bù失shī其qí時shí

jiàn jī ér zuò bù shī qí shí

see the opportunity and act, do not lose the moment

Act when you see the right opportunity; do not let the right moment slip by.

Opportunity does not linger like a houseguest waiting to be invited in. It passes at its own pace and the window of catching it is exactly as wide as the speed of recognition followed by action. The one who sees it and considers whether to act usually considers past the point where acting was possible.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Yi Jing 易經·繫辭下 (Xì Cí II)

Read the full reading
57

三sān年nián不bù飛fēi，一yī飛fēi沖chōng天tiān

sān nián bù fēi yī fēi chōng tiān

three years no flying, one flight pierce the sky

After a long period of silence or preparation, make a spectacular and lasting achievement.

The great bird grounds itself for three years while the smaller ones chirp and fly about in their daily hurry. It is not asleep but accumulating something that cannot be rushed. The sky is patient, and so is the bird that is preparing to split it. One flight, when the time is right, is enough.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 火 Fire 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Shi Ji 史記·滑稽列傳 (Huájī biography); also Zuozhuan

Read the full reading
58

飛fēi鳥niǎo盡jìn，良liáng弓gōng藏cáng

fēi niǎo jìn liáng gōng cáng

birds all gone, good bow put away

When a goal is accomplished, those who helped achieve it are cast aside; the tool discarded once its use is done.

The bow that brought down every bird hangs in the dark cabinet now that the sky is empty. Usefulness, when tied to a single purpose, ends with that purpose. Those who serve with versatility rather than singularity of function tend to remain in the light after the immediate task is done.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks back 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Shi Ji 史記·越王勾踐世家 (Yuè Wáng Gōujiàn biography)

Read the full reading
59

凡fán事shì預yù則zé立lì，不bù預yù則zé廢fèi

fán shì yù zé lì bù yù zé fèi

all matters prepared then stand, not prepared then fail

Planning before action leads to success; lack of planning leads to failure.

The tent that stands through the storm was pegged into the ground before the wind came, not during it. All the scrambling that happens after the event that was not planned for is the expensive education that preparation would have made unnecessary. Forethought is not pessimism; it is the practical form of hope.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Li Ji 禮記·中庸 (Zhōng Yōng, Doctrine of the Mean)

Read the full reading
60

守shǒu得dé雲yún開kāi見jiàn月yuè明míng

shǒu dé yún kāi jiàn yuè míng

hold on until the clouds part and the bright moon appears

Endure through the dark long enough and the clear, luminous moment will come.

The sky has not cleared, and you are tired of being told it will. But cloud is not weather that stays; it is weather that passes over someone who waits underneath it. Keep your place. The moon does not vanish when it is hidden — it is only being patient with you.

詩詞 Verse Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Verse associated with 水滸傳 (Water Margin) and the folk 勸世賢文; late Yuan / Ming

Read the full reading
61

寧nìng可kě信xìn其qí有yǒu，不bù可kě信xìn其qí無wú

nìng kě xìn qí yǒu bù kě xìn qí wú

better to believe it exists than to believe it does not

When in doubt, take the safer, more cautious option; better safe than sorry.

The bridge whose stability is uncertain does not reward the confidence of the person who crosses it without checking. Where the outcome of being wrong is serious, the asymmetry argues for the cautious reading. To act as though the danger might be real costs you the time of checking; to act as though it is not real could cost you everything else.

諺語 Proverb Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common in cautious decision-making contexts)

Read the full reading
62

防fáng患huàn於yú未wèi然rán

fáng huàn yú wèi rán

prevent disaster before it is thus

Prevent trouble before it happens; nip it in the bud.

The physician praised for curing illness is famous; the one who prevents it is invisible. Yet the invisible work is the greater one. Those who act before the problem arrives leave no dramatic story, only a quiet, unbroken life.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Looks ahead 水 Water 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Han Shu 漢書·霍光傳 (Huò Guāng biography)

Read the full reading
63

渾hún水shuǐ摸mō魚yú

hún shuǐ mō yú

fish in muddy water

Profit from disorder or confusion; exploit a chaotic situation for personal gain.

The fish cannot be seen in clouded water, and the hand that knows this reaches in where a clear view would have warned it off. Turbulence has its beneficiaries, and they are usually the ones who created the turbulence. Clear water is the friend of the honest and the adversary of those who prefer invisibility.

成語 Adage Timing & Fortune's Turning Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
64

冰bīng非fēi一yī日rì之zhī寒hán

bīng fēi yī rì zhī hán

ice is not cold from a single day

Nothing reaches its current state overnight; all things develop gradually.

The ice that now covers the river completely did not arrive with one cold night. Each night contributed what it could, imperceptibly, until one morning the surface was solid enough to hold weight. What appears as a sudden condition is the sum of a hundred quiet contributions. Never mistake the final stage for a recent one.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Looks back 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb

Read the full reading
65

少shào壯zhuàng不bù努nǔ力lì，老lǎo大dà徒tú傷shāng悲bēi

shào zhuàng bù nǔ lì lǎo dà tú shāng bēi

young and strong not striving, old age only grief

Those who do not work hard in youth can only grieve in old age.

The hands that are strong today will not always be strong, and the mind quickened by youth does not promise to stay quick. The effort not made in the season of capacity does not wait patiently for another season; it simply does not happen. Act now, while the tool is sharp.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 木 Wood 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Han Music Bureau 漢樂府·《長歌行》 (Cháng Gē Xíng, Long Song Ballad)

Read the full reading
66

日rì積jī月yuè累lěi

rì jī yuè lěi

day accumulates, month piles

Through daily and monthly accumulation, great things are built; small consistent efforts compound over time.

Nothing looks like much on the day it is done. It is only when you turn around after a year that you see the distance covered. The work does not announce itself — it simply appears one morning as a life you built without noticing.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 土 Earth 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Common Chinese idiom; widely used since Tang dynasty

Read the full reading
67

滴dī水shuǐ穿chuān石shí

dī shuǐ chuān shí

dripping water pierces stone

Persistent effort can accomplish seemingly impossible things.

The drop does not strike the stone with force but with frequency, arriving again and again at the same point without resentment or hurry. Stone is hard and water is soft and still the water wins in the end, by refusing to be anywhere else. This is what patience looks like when you give it enough time.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Zhan Guo Ce 戰國策; also common Chinese folk proverb

Read the full reading
68

一yī山shān放fàng過guò一yī山shān攔lán

yī shān fàng guò yī shān lán

one mountain let past, one mountain blocks

Obstacles follow one another on the road of life; overcome one difficulty, and another appears.

The traveler who passes one mountain discovers the next mountain, and the discovery is no longer a surprise but a familiar feature of all roads that go somewhere worth going. Each mountain passed is confirmation that mountains can be passed. The road that would be easy if there were only one obstacle is a different kind of road entirely.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Yang Wanli 楊萬里·《過松源晨炊漆公店》 (Song Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
69

一yī步bù一yī個gè腳jiǎo印yìn

yī bù yī gè jiǎo yìn

one step, one footprint

Every step leaves a mark; make progress steadily and concretely, one step at a time.

The path made of real steps is legible in the earth as a trail of evidence that someone truly passed this way. To move without leaving a mark is to have passed without passing. Each step taken with full weight and full intention becomes part of the ground itself, and the accumulation is called a life.

俗語 Saying Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
70

一yī而ér再zài，再zài而ér三sān

yī ér zài zài ér sān

once, and again, again and three times

Do something again and again; persist repeatedly.

Three times is not an arbitrary number; it is the threshold where repetition becomes intention. The first time is accident, the second is coincidence, and the third time is a pattern being established or a determination being demonstrated. The one who comes back a third time has decided something the first and second time could not have proven.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Zuozhuan 左傳·莊公十年 (Zhuāng Gōng Year 10)

Read the full reading
71

不bù怕pà慢màn，只zhǐ怕pà站zhàn

bù pà màn zhǐ pà zhàn

not afraid of slow, only afraid of standing still

It doesn't matter if you go slowly, as long as you keep moving; stop and you lose everything.

Slowness preserves the direction; stopping abandons it. The snail arrives while the rabbit that sat down does not, and the only catastrophe along the road is not insufficient speed but the decision to stay. Any pace that maintains the direction will eventually cover the distance. The only unforgivable pace is no pace at all.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
72

不bù積jī跬kuǐ步bù，無wú以yǐ至zhì千qiān里lǐ

bù jī kuǐ bù wú yǐ zhì qiān lǐ

without accumulating half-steps, impossible to reach a thousand li

Without accumulating small steps, you cannot reach great distances; every great achievement is built from small ones.

The half-step is not half a real step; it is a real step that simply did not complete itself. And yet even the half-step, taken a thousand times, builds a distance that the person who waits for the full and ready stride never covers. The small and incomplete movement, faithfully repeated, is the actual engine of all long distance. Do not wait for the whole step.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Xun Zi 荀子·勸學 (Quàn Xué, Encouraging Learning)

Read the full reading
73

大dà器qì晚wǎn成chéng

dà qì wǎn chéng

great vessel forms late

The greatest achievements take time to develop; late bloomers often surpass those who peak early.

The tree that will hold the most weight grew the slowest. The person who looked like they were falling behind was simply growing roots the others skipped. There is no early arrival for what is meant to last.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 41 (Laozi); popularized in Han dynasty usage

Read the full reading
74

忍rěn辱rǔ負fù重zhòng

rěn rǔ fù zhòng

endure humiliation, carry heavy burden

To endure disgrace and bear heavy responsibility for a greater purpose.

Some loads are carried not because they are fair but because someone must carry them, and you are the one who can. The indignity of the moment is real and the purpose behind it is also real; both can be true at once. What is borne with grace becomes, over time, something like strength.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source San Guo Zhi 三國志·蜀志·諸葛亮傳 (Zhūgě Liàng biography)

Read the full reading
75

愚yú公gōng移yí山shān

yú gōng yí shān

foolish old man moves mountain

Persistence and determination can overcome any obstacle, no matter how large.

He was old and the mountain was old and everyone watching agreed that nothing would change. He disagreed and picked up his shovel. The children's children would carry the work forward, and the mountain, which had no opinion on the matter, eventually agreed to move. What the world calls stubborn, time sometimes calls right.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Liezi 列子·湯問 (Tāng Wèn chapter)

Read the full reading
76

臥wò薪xīn嘗cháng膽dǎn，十shí年nián雪xuě劍jiàn

wò xīn cháng dǎn shí nián xuě jiàn

sleep on firewood taste gall, ten years to polish the sword

Endure hardship and humiliation for years to prepare for ultimate achievement.

He slept on sticks and tasted bitterness each morning so that the comfortable feeling would not return before the work was done. Self-imposed difficulty is not masochism but calibration: keeping the edge sharp enough to remember what was lost and what must be recovered. Ten years is the price, and it is paid quietly, day by day.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 金 Metal 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Shi Ji 史記·越王勾踐世家 (Yuè Wáng Gōujiàn biography)

Read the full reading
77

自zì力lì更gèng生shēng

zì lì gèng shēng

self-strength, further life

Regenerate through one's own strength; self-reliance and independence.

The tree that has grown its own root system in hard soil does not topple when the neighboring tree is cut down. Self-reliance is not pride or isolation but the quiet confidence of knowing that the source of what you need is within reach of your own hands. This is what makes independence a form of freedom, not aloneness.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 木 Wood 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Modern Chinese political idiom; rooted in classical self-cultivation tradition

Read the full reading
78

不bù到dào長cháng城chéng非fēi好hǎo漢hàn

bù dào cháng chéng fēi hǎo hàn

not reaching the Great Wall is not a true hero

One must strive to reach the ultimate goal to call themselves great.

The Great Wall sits at the limit of what the legs will consider and then demands more. It is not a sight but a test, the mountain that asks whether the legs and the will are prepared to be accountable to each other. Those who arrive have already answered the question that most people only carry.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 土 Earth 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Mao Zedong 毛澤東·《清平樂·六盤山》 (Qīng Píng Yuè, Six Ridges Mountain, 1935)

Read the full reading
79

天tiān下xià無wú難nán事shì，只zhǐ怕pà有yǒu心xīn人rén

tiān xià wú nán shì zhǐ pà yǒu xīn rén

under heaven no difficult matters, only fear people with heart

Nothing in the world is too difficult for the determined person.

The obstacle is only absolute to those who have already decided to stop. For the person who has truly decided to move, the thing that seemed impossible begins to reveal its seams, its handles, the place where a shoulder can be applied. Intention is not magic; it is pressure sustained past the point where most people rest.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Qing Dynasty 清·李汝珍《鏡花緣》 (Jìng Huā Yuán, Flowers in the Mirror)

Read the full reading
80

有yǒu志zhì者zhě，事shì竟jìng成chéng

yǒu zhì zhě shì jìng chéng

one who has determination, matters will ultimately succeed

Where there is a will, there is a way; with true resolve, any task will eventually be accomplished.

The task does not care how many times you have failed. It only cares whether you have stopped. The person with genuine resolve does not count the failures — they count them only as the necessary steps between where they started and where they are going.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Book of Later Han 後漢書, Geng Yan biography (耿弇傳)

Read the full reading
81

有yǒu志zhì者zhě事shì竟jìng成chéng

yǒu zhì zhě shì jìng chéng

those who have will, matters ultimately succeed

Where there is a will, there is a way; those with determination ultimately succeed.

The will is not a feeling but a decision that outlasts the feeling. When the feeling has gone cold and the body wants to quit, the will is what is still standing at the gate. Determination does not guarantee a smooth road but it guarantees arrival, eventually, if you do not turn back.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Hou Han Shu 後漢書·耿弇傳 (Gěng Yǎn biography)

Read the full reading
82

男nán兒ér當dāng自zì強qiáng

nán ér dāng zì qiáng

a man should self-strengthen

One should always strive to better oneself through one's own effort.

The strength that comes from within is the only kind that cannot be taken. External supports are helpful but conditional; the internal resource is available in the dark, in the alone, in the exact moment when nothing from outside is coming. Build that. Everything else is supplement.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Li Wenying 李文瑛 (Cantonese opera/traditional Chinese cultural lyric)

Read the full reading
83

矢shǐ志zhì不bù渝yú

shǐ zhì bù yú

arrow aspiration does not change

Unswerving determination; hold to one's purpose without deviation.

The arrow once loosed from the bow does not change its mind about its direction. Commitment made with this quality of intention does not renegotiate when the weather changes or the path becomes obscured. The direction was decided at the moment of the loosing, and everything since then is just the flight.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in heroic and literary contexts)

Read the full reading
84

精jīng誠chéng所suǒ至zhì，金jīn石shí為wéi開kāi

jīng chéng suǒ zhì jīn shí wéi kāi

where pure sincerity reaches, gold and stone open

Absolute sincerity can move even the hardest things; genuine dedication overcomes all obstacles.

The archer who practiced for years to hit the stone target eventually shot an arrow that buried itself into granite, because every fiber of his intention was aimed in the same direction at once. When sincerity reaches that concentration, gold and stone are no longer the hardest things in the field. The softest power, fully gathered, overcomes the hardest matter.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Hou Han Shu 後漢書·廣陵思王荊傳 (Guǎng Líng Sī Wáng Jīng biography)

Read the full reading
85

虎hǔ頭tóu蛇shé尾wěi

hǔ tóu shé wěi

tiger head, snake tail

A strong start and a weak finish; beginning boldly but ending feebly.

The tiger's head is magnificent and the snake's tail is not, and the thing that promises one and delivers the other has done something worse than simply starting badly. The loss of the grand opening is compounded by the reminder of what was originally promised. Finish what you began with the same quality you brought at the start.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Yuan Dynasty 元·康進之《李逵負荊》 (Lǐ Kuí Fù Jīng, play)

Read the full reading
86

循xún序xù漸jiàn進jìn

xún xù jiàn jìn

follow order, gradually advance

Proceed step by step in proper order; make gradual progress.

The staircase does not ask you to fly; it only asks you to lift your foot one step higher than before. Order is not a cage but a kindness, preserving energy for the long climb. Those who skip steps find the missing ones waiting somewhere ahead.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Zhu Xi 朱熹·《近思錄》 (Jìn Sī Lù, Reflections on Things at Hand)

Read the full reading
87

百bǎi尺chǐ竿gān頭tóu，更gèng進jìn一yī步bù

bǎi chǐ gān tóu gèng jìn yī bù

at the top of a hundred-foot pole, still advance one more step

Even at the peak of achievement, strive one step further; keep improving.

The top of the pole is not the end but a platform for the next gesture, and the one who thinks reaching it means stopping has confused arrival with completion. The invitation at every summit is the same: one more step. This is not ambition's restlessness but growth's natural appetite for what comes after the view.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Song Dynasty 宋·釋道原《景德傳燈錄》 (Jǐng Dé Chuán Dēng Lù, Buddhist text)

Read the full reading
88

自zì強qiáng不bù息xī

zì qiáng bù xī

self-strengthen without ceasing

Constantly strive to improve oneself; self-improvement through ceaseless effort.

The sky turns without resting, and the noble person takes this as their pattern. Not the desperate striver who burns out, but the one whose motion is simply continuous, the way the earth continues to turn without announcing it. Self-improvement that does not stop is not ambition but breathing, the most natural rhythm of the alive.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Yi Jing 易經·乾卦·象 (Qián Guà, Heaven Hexagram Commentary)

Read the full reading
89

千qiān里lǐ之zhī行xíng，始shǐ於yú足zú下xià

qiān lǐ zhī xíng shǐ yú zú xià

a thousand-li road begins beneath the feet

Every great undertaking starts with a single step.

The far place frightens only from a distance. Stand at your own threshold, take the single step in front of you, and the thousand miles begin to belong to you.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 土 Earth 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經, ch. 64 (Laozi)

Read the full reading
90

得dé過guò且qiě過guò

dé guò qiě guò

manage to get by and get by

Just muddle through; do only what is necessary to get by without aspiring to more.

The bird that can only pass the day is the kingfisher in the story who, asked how he had survived the winter, replied only that he had crossed each day as it came. There is something to admire in the bare management of survival, but the one who has more capacity available and uses only this much is a different story, and it knows it.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 土 Earth 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Ming Dynasty 明·馮夢龍《醒世恆言》 (Xǐng Shì Héng Yán)

Read the full reading
91

水shuǐ滴dī石shí穿chuān

shuǐ dī shí chuān

water drop, stone pierced

Constant effort, no matter how small, will eventually overcome any obstacle.

The stone is indifferent and massive and the drop is soft and small, and the drop wins not on any given day but over all the days together. There is a geometry to persistence that force cannot access: repetition bends what impact cannot break. Show up again. That is the whole strategy.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Song Dynasty 宋·羅大經《鶴林玉露》 (Hè Lín Yù Lù, Luo Dajing)

Read the full reading
92

人rén生shēng路lù漫màn漫màn

rén shēng lù màn màn

life's road long and winding

Life's path is long and winding; the journey of life is extended and not easily completed.

The road does not shorten for those who wish it shorter or lengthen for those who wish more time. It is exactly as long as a life is long, and every bend withholds what is around it until you arrive. The person who accepts the length of the road as given rather than arguing with it travels more comfortably, and often, somehow, arrives not later but in better condition.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 水 Water 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk expression (common in literary and social contexts)

Read the full reading
93

千qiān山shān萬wàn水shuǐ

qiān shān wàn shuǐ

thousand mountains, ten thousand waters

Over countless mountains and rivers; through great distances and many difficulties.

Thousand mountains and ten thousand rivers is not hyperbole but a description of what love and loyalty are willing to cross. The one who says they will come through that is committing not to an easy route but to the refusal of any distance as a final argument against arrival. The journey is exactly as long as the devotion is deep.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 水 Water 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying and literary metaphor

Read the full reading
94

留liú下xià腳jiǎo印yìn方fāng稱chēng路lù

liú xià jiǎo yìn fāng chēng lù

leave behind footprints, only then called a road

A road is made by those who walk it; the path is defined by the act of traveling it.

The road that no one has walked is not yet a road but a possibility. What defines it as a road is the accumulated evidence of passage: the pressed grass, the worn earth, the route recognizable because others have already chosen it. The first person who makes the choice creates, unknowingly, an invitation for everyone who follows.

諺語 Proverb Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
95

老lǎo驥jì伏fú櫪lì，志zhì在zài千qiān里lǐ

lǎo jì fú lì zhì zài qiān lǐ

old thoroughbred lies in stall, ambition at a thousand li

Even in old age, the great still harbor great ambitions.

The horse in the stall whose racing days are behind it still lifts its head at the sound of the starting drum. Age can slow the body without slowing the aspiration that lives deeper than the body. The distance is still in there, burning quietly, and it does not depend on legs.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Cao Cao 曹操·《步出夏門行》 (Bù Chū Xià Mén Xíng poem)

Read the full reading
96

路lù漫màn漫màn其qí修xiū遠yuǎn兮xī

lù màn màn qí xiū yuǎn xī

road long long its path far indeed

The road is long and the journey far; one must keep searching and striving.

The poet stood on the road and did not lie about its length. He said it stretches beyond what the eye can hold, and still he chose to walk. Honesty about the distance is not discouragement but preparation for a commitment made with open eyes. The long road walked willingly is different from one walked by surprise.

詩詞 Verse Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 木 Wood 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Qu Yuan 屈原·《離騷》 (Lí Sāo, Encountering Sorrow)

Read the full reading
97

聞wén雞jī起qǐ舞wǔ

wén jī qǐ wǔ

hear the rooster and rise to train

To wake at the first crow and begin, a diligence that starts before the day asks for it.

The crow that wakes the yard is a summons you have learned to love. You are most yourself in the first hour, when the discipline belongs to you alone and the day has made no demands yet.

成語 Adage Perseverance & the Long Road Looks ahead 木 Wood 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Book of Jin 晉書, Zu Ti 祖逖傳

Read the full reading
98

人rén心xīn齊qí，泰tài山shān移yí

rén xīn qí tài shān yí

people's hearts united, Mount Tai moves

When people are united in purpose, even the greatest obstacles can be overcome.

The stone that no single pair of hands can lift yields to ten pairs working as one, and the mountain that defeats the individual yields to the village that agrees. Unity is not the absence of difference but the presence of a shared direction large enough to contain the differences. Mountains move slowly, but they move.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb

Read the full reading
99

寸cùn土tǔ必bì爭zhēng

cùn tǔ bì zhēng

every inch of soil must be contested

Fight for every inch of ground; leave nothing undefended.

The inch that is yielded without contention was never truly yours, and the inch yielded from a position of full awareness and choice is a different thing: a gift, a strategy, or a boundary wisely released. The question is always whether you are relinquishing with knowledge or simply failing to notice what you are giving away.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese military and political saying

Read the full reading
100

一yī以yǐ當dāng萬wàn

yī yǐ dāng wàn

one person equals ten thousand

One exceptional person or principle can handle what ten thousand cannot; quality over quantity.

The single right answer cuts through ten thousand wrong ones as though they were not there. What is truly superior does not need to overpower; it needs only to be clearly itself in the presence of everything else. The one who truly understands the matter does not need ten thousand allies to be heard.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Shi Ji 史記·項羽本紀 (Xiàng Yǔ biography)

Read the full reading
101

一yī刀dāo兩liǎng斷duàn

yī dāo liǎng duàn

one knife, two halves

Make a clean break; cut decisively and completely without hesitation.

The clean cut through the difficult situation produces two clear halves and a clear ending. The jagged cut that hesitates leaves edges that catch and tear on everything that follows. Decisiveness is not harshness but precision, and the clean ending — painful as it is — gives both sides something they can work with: a boundary that is actually a boundary.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in conflict and decision contexts)

Read the full reading
102

一yī山shān不bù容róng二èr虎hǔ

yī shān bù róng èr hǔ

one mountain does not hold two tigers

Two rivals of great strength rarely share a single territory.

You were not built to share a peak. The pull to hold ground that is wholly yours reads as pride only from the outside; from within, it is the tiger knowing that some strengths need their own mountain to move at full stride.

俗語 Saying Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 木 Wood 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source folk proverb 諺語

Read the full reading
103

不bù入rù虎hǔ穴xué，焉yān得dé虎hǔ子zǐ

bù rù hǔ xué yān dé hǔ zǐ

without entering the tiger's den, how to get the tiger's cub

Nothing ventured, nothing gained; you must take risks to achieve great things.

The tiger cub is in the den, and the den is where the tiger lives, and this is the exact information about why the cub is valuable and why it requires courage to obtain. What is worth having tends to live in places that require something from those who want it. The wanting without the going is simply daydreaming about tiger cubs.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Hou Han Shu 後漢書·班超傳 (Bān Chāo biography)

Read the full reading
104

殺shā一yī儆jǐng百bǎi

shā yī jǐng bǎi

execute one to warn a hundred

Make an example of one to deter many others; use a single severe case to prevent broader problems.

The single clear consequence, made visible to the many, does the work that a hundred private warnings could not. This is not cruelty but the economics of attention: the one case that becomes the lesson saves the hundred cases that would otherwise require the same lesson at greater cost. Examples are how standards become real.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Han Shu 漢書·尹翁歸傳 (Yǐn Wēngguī biography)

Read the full reading
105

毛máo遂suì自zì薦jiàn

máo suì zì jiàn

Mao Sui recommends himself

Recommend oneself for a role or task; bold self-promotion at the right moment.

Mao Sui had waited three years in the prince's retinue without being noticed, and when the moment arrived where his skill could be of use, he did not wait to be chosen. He offered himself plainly and the prince's resistance dissolved when the offer proved true. Sometimes the door must be knocked on by the one who needs it open.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Shi Ji 史記·平原君虞卿列傳 (Píngyuán Jūn biography)

Read the full reading
106

沉chén舟zhōu破pò釜fǔ，百bǎi二èr秦qín關guān

chén zhōu pò fǔ bǎi èr qín guān

sink the boats, smash the pots, a hundred and two Qin passes

Burn one's bridges to force total commitment; the willingness to destroy retreat leads to great victory.

The army that burned its boats at the shore had nothing to think about but forward. Fear of losing the retreat was converted, in one act of destruction, into the pure force of no alternative. Sometimes the obstacle to full commitment is the path back, and removing it is not recklessness but strategy.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Shi Ji 史記·項羽本紀 (Xiàng Yǔ biography)

Read the full reading
107

獨dú當dāng一yī面miàn

dú dāng yī miàn

alone managing one front

Hold one's own on an entire front alone; independently take charge of a major area.

There is a particular kind of test in which the person stands alone on the sector they were given and discovers whether the combination of preparation, will, and intelligence is sufficient without help. Those who can manage their own front do not merely survive it; they leave it better than they found it, as proof that they were the right person for the position.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source San Guo Zhi 三國志·蜀志·諸葛亮傳 (Zhūgě Liàng biography)

Read the full reading
108

義yì之zhī所suǒ在zài，雖suī千qiān萬wàn人rén吾wú往wǎng矣yǐ

yì zhī suǒ zài suī qiān wàn rén wú wǎng yǐ

where righteousness lies, though ten thousand stand against me, I go forward

Where righteousness demands, go forward without hesitation, regardless of opposition.

The ten thousand standing in the way are real and their opposition is noted and the person with genuine conviction walks forward anyway. This is not recklessness or stubbornness but the arithmetic of values: when what is right and what is popular diverge, the person of conviction has already chosen. The weight of numbers does not change the weight of what is true.

詩詞 Verse Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Mencius 孟子·公孫丑上 (Gōng Sūn Chǒu I)

Read the full reading
109

背bèi水shuǐ一yī戰zhàn

bèi shuǐ yī zhàn

back to water, one battle

Fight with one's back to the river; make a last stand with no possibility of retreat.

General Han Xin placed his army at the river's edge with the enemy in front and the current behind, and the men discovered that they had only one direction available to them: forward. The removal of the option of flight is also the removal of the anxiety that flight creates. Sometimes constraint is the most motivating arrangement possible.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 水 Water 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Shi Ji 史記·淮陰侯列傳 (Huáiyīn Hóu biography)

Read the full reading
110

臨lín危wēi不bù懼jù

lín wēi bù jù

face danger without fear

Face peril without flinching; remain composed in the face of danger.

Fear is useful information about a threat; terror is fear that has taken over the operation. The one who faces danger without being consumed by fear is not without feeling but in possession of a stillness that thinks more clearly than panic. Composure in danger is not absence of alarm but mastery of the response to it.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in heroic literature)

Read the full reading
111

見jiàn義yì勇yǒng為wéi

jiàn yì yǒng wéi

see righteousness, bravely act

When you see what is right, act on it with courage.

Righteousness seen and not acted upon is still a kind of blindness. The moment of recognition is also the moment of obligation, and the body that moves toward it without hesitating has already decided what kind of person it is. Courage is often just refusal to overthink.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 木 Wood 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·為政 (Wèi Zhèng II)

Read the full reading
112

人rén無wú遠yuǎn慮lǜ，必bì有yǒu近jìn憂yōu

rén wú yuǎn lǜ bì yǒu jìn yōu

a person without long-range plans will inevitably have near-term worries

Failure to plan for the future creates problems in the present; long-range thinking prevents short-range crises.

The worry that arrives today was usually planted some time ago by an absence of thought. The person who spent last spring thinking about winter does not scramble in November. The one who did not think about it does — and calls it bad luck.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Analects 論語, Book 15 (Wei Ling Gong 衛靈公, ch. 12)

Read the full reading
113

會huì當dāng凌líng絕jué頂dǐng，一yī覽lǎn眾zhòng山shān小xiǎo

huì dāng líng jué dǐng yī lǎn zhòng shān xiǎo

should reach the very summit, one gaze makes all mountains small

Reach the highest point and all other heights look small; supreme achievement commands all perspective.

The summit is not arrogance but arrival, and the view from it does not diminish the other mountains but reveals their proper scale. What seemed enormous from below becomes context from above, and context is the gift that the climb earns. Rise, and let the panorama teach you proportion.

詩詞 Verse Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Du Fu 杜甫·《望嶽》 (Wàng Yuè, Looking at Mount Tai, Tang Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
114

有yǒu備bèi無wú患huàn

yǒu bèi wú huàn

having prepared, no calamity

Proper preparation prevents disaster; those who plan ahead face no unforeseen trouble.

The roof does not leak in the dry season. It is only when the rain arrives that you learn whether someone was thinking ahead. Preparation is the quiet form of courage — the choice made on the calm day for the benefit of the stormy one.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Zuozhuan 左傳, Duke Xiang year 11 (襄公十一年)

Read the full reading
115

疾jí風fēng暴bào雨yǔ

jí fēng bào yǔ

fierce wind violent rain

A sudden, violent storm; a rapid and forceful situation or action.

The storm does not announce its arrival with a gentle curtsy; it arrives and the world reorganizes around its energy. Some situations require the same: the entry that does not build to itself but arrives already at full force, taking the field before the field has had time to prepare a response. Swift and complete is sometimes the only effective form.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Zhuangzi 莊子·天道 (Tiān Dào, The Way of Heaven)

Read the full reading
116

龍lóng飛fēi鳳fèng舞wǔ

lóng fēi fèng wǔ

dragon flies, phoenix dances

Lively and vigorous; powerful and graceful movement; also used for fine calligraphy.

The dragon does not apologize for the scale of its flight, and the phoenix does not contain its color for the benefit of the gray sky. When power and grace move together in a single form, the result is something the eye cannot stop tracking. This is what excellence looks like when it is not self-conscious: abundant, moving, and entirely itself.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Song Dynasty 宋·蘇軾《表忠觀碑》 (Biǎo Zhōng Guān Bēi)

Read the full reading
117

居jū安ān思sī危wēi

jū ān sī wēi

dwelling in peace, think of danger

In times of comfort and safety, do not forget to prepare for difficulty; complacency invites disaster.

The house that feels most safe is the one where someone, once, thought about what could go wrong. Not out of fear — out of care. Peaceful seasons are when the roof should be checked, the stores replenished, the path out of the valley cleared.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Zuozhuan 左傳, Duke Xiang year 11 (襄公十一年); Shu Xiang's advice

Read the full reading
118

一yī片piàn丹dān心xīn照zhào日rì月yuè

yī piàn dān xīn zhào rì yuè

one piece of red heart illuminates sun and moon

Sincere and passionate loyalty that shines like the sun and moon; noble and pure patriotism.

The red heart does not ask whether the sun and moon are watching. It shines its loyalty because that is its nature, as constant and sourceless as the light it is compared to. Dedication that reaches this quality becomes, in time, its own kind of illumination — visible even when the person who holds it is gone.

詩詞 Verse Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Wen Tianxiang 文天祥·《過零丁洋》 (Guò Líng Dīng Yáng, Passing Lingding Ocean, Song Dynasty)

Read the full reading
119

地dì上shàng本běn沒méi有yǒu路lù

dì shàng běn méi yǒu lù

on the earth, there was originally no road

Roads are made by those who walk them; the pioneer creates paths for others to follow.

The first person to cross the field created the impression of a track. The second person followed it, and the third, and after enough feet the track became the path and after enough years the path became the road. What looks like infrastructure was once simply someone walking where no one had walked. The pioneer is the builder of paths by the act of crossing.

詩詞 Verse Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Lu Xun 魯迅·《故鄉》 (Gù Xiāng, My Old Home, 1921)

Read the full reading
120

疾jí如rú風fēng，徐xú如rú林lín

jí rú fēng xú rú lín

swift as wind, patient as forest

Move swiftly when swift, and slowly and steadily when steadiness is called for; adapt one's pace to the situation.

The strategy is not always speed or always patience but the wisdom to know which one the moment requires and to apply it without argument or delay. The forest is patient without weakness and the wind is swift without recklessness. Both are available to the one who reads the situation well enough to choose correctly.

詩詞 Verse Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 木 Wood 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Sun Zi 孫子兵法·軍爭 (Jūn Zhēng, Maneuvering); popularized in Fūrinkazan

Read the full reading
121

眾zhòng人rén拾shí柴chái火huǒ焰yàn高gāo

zhòng rén shí chái huǒ yàn gāo

many people gather firewood, flames rise high

Many hands make light work; collective effort makes great achievement.

One log smolders alone and barely holds a flame. Three logs breathe together and you have a fire that cooks and warms and holds back the dark. Every contribution, however small, changes the height of what is possible. This is why gathering together is itself a form of action.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
122

破pò釜fǔ沉chén舟zhōu

pò fǔ chén zhōu

break the cauldrons, sink the boats

Commit fully with no line of retreat.

There is a kind of decision you keep half-making, leaving one boat tied at the bank so you can row back to who you were. You already know that boat is the reason you have not crossed. Burn it, and the far shore stops being a maybe.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Records of the Grand Historian 史記

Read the full reading
123

龍lóng馬mǎ精jīng神shén

lóng mǎ jīng shén

dragon horse spirit

The vigorous and spirited energy of a dragon-horse; extraordinary vitality and ambition.

The dragon-horse of Chinese legend moved with the strength of both creatures simultaneously, carrying the chi of the mythic and the practicality of the animal together in one magnificent motion. To embody this is to be both visionary and effective, both inspiring and actually moving. This is the spirit that makes the world take notice.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese auspicious saying (common in celebrations and blessings)

Read the full reading
124

人rén爭zhēng一yī口kǒu氣qì，佛fó爭zhēng一yī柱zhù香xiāng

rén zhēng yī kǒu qì fó zhēng yī zhù xiāng

person contests for one breath of spirit, Buddha contests for one stick of incense

People fight for dignity and honor; even Buddha competes for devotion — everyone has their pride.

The single breath of spirit worth fighting for is not pride in the petty sense but the refusal to be treated as less than one is. Every person has this — a core that will not be reduced without resistance — and even the most gentle and devoted practice retains its own kind of integrity. Dignity is not optional, not even for the enlightened.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common in social wisdom)

Read the full reading
125

圍wéi魏wèi救jiù趙zhào

wéi wèi jiù zhào

surround Wei to rescue Zhao

Attack what an enemy values to force them to withdraw; solve a problem indirectly.

The direct road to Zhao ran through the Wei army, which was why General Sun Bin did not take it. He turned instead and marched on Wei's capital, and Wei's army turned around to defend what it could not afford to lose.
