The oblique approach is not cowardice but geometry: the best angle is rarely the frontal one.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Shi Ji 史記·孫子吳起列傳 (Sūn Zǐ Wú Qǐ biography)

Read the full reading
126

殺shā雞jī儆jǐng猴hóu

shā jī jǐng hóu

kill the chicken to warn the monkey

Punish one to warn others; use an example to discourage others from similar behavior.

The lesson is meant for the watching, not only the one who receives it. Order is sometimes maintained not by constant enforcement but by memorable example, a single clear signal that travels further than any repeated warning. The monkey that watches understands what cannot be explained.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese proverb (common in political and management contexts)

Read the full reading
127

瞞mán天tiān過guò海hǎi

mán tiān guò hǎi

deceive heaven, cross the sea

Use a deceptive strategy to accomplish a goal; cross the sea by deceiving the sky.

Emperor Taizong would not cross the sea, convinced he had a phobia of water, so his generals built an elaborate illusion — a palace on boats — and he crossed without knowing he was crossing. The water was not the obstacle; the fear of water was, and the fear was managed with misdirection. Sometimes the method of doing the necessary thing is theater.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 水 Water 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Sun Zi 孫子兵法·三十六計 (Thirty-Six Stratagems, First Stratagem)

Read the full reading
128

謀móu勝shèng於yú無wú形xíng之zhī前qián

móu shèng yú wú xíng zhī qián

strategy wins before form exists

Victory is decided before the battle is visible; superior planning precedes action.

The great general wins in the room before the generals even meet on the field. What looks like decisive action is usually the final expression of a decision already made in the quiet of preparation. Those who seem to act brilliantly in the moment have usually been planning brilliantly long before the moment.

詩詞 Verse Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 金 Metal 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Sun Zi 孫子·謀攻 (Móu Gōng, Planning Attack)

Read the full reading
129

一yī人rén做zuò事shì一yī人rén當dāng

yī rén zuò shì yī rén dāng

one person does the matter, one person takes responsibility

Whoever does something must take responsibility for it; stand up for what you've done.

The act and its responsibility belong to the same person and cannot be separated by clever argument or convenient amnesia. To act and then step back from the consequence is a form of theft from the social fabric that holds everyone together. What you do is yours; let that be a reason to act carefully and own it completely.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (common in folk and literary contexts)

Read the full reading
130

一yī鳴míng驚jīng人rén

yī míng jīng rén

one cry startles people

One action or word that shocks everyone; a sudden display of remarkable ability.

The bird that has been silent in the reeds for three years has not been idle. It has been listening to the shape of the wind, feeling the tension of the right moment through its feathers. When it finally calls, the whole marsh remembers.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 火 Fire 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Shi Ji 史記·滑稽列傳 (Huájī biography); also Zuozhuan 左傳

Read the full reading
131

一yī鼓gǔ作zuò氣qì

yī gǔ zuò qì

one drumbeat rouses the spirit

Finish in one sustained burst while morale is at its peak.

Your courage has a first wind and it is stronger than you trust it to be. If you pause to gather yourself a second and third time, you are not resting, you are letting the drum go quiet. Move now, while the first beat is still in your chest.

成語 Adage Courage & Decisive Action Looks ahead 火 Fire 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Zuo Zhuan 左傳

Read the full reading
132

寧nìng為wéi雞jī頭tóu，不bù為wéi牛niú後hòu

nìng wéi jī tóu bù wéi niú hòu

rather be the head of a chicken than the tail of an ox

Better to be a leader of something small than a follower at the tail of something large.

The rooster's head has the view, the decision, and the responsibility; the ox's tail has the flies and the obligation to follow. Scale matters less than position, and the domain small enough to be understood and led is worth more than the large domain where you are only one of the indistinguishable back legs. Lead what you can actually lead.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Warring States 戰國策·韓策 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Han Strategies)

Read the full reading
133

燕yàn雀què安ān知zhī鴻hóng鵠hú之zhī志zhì

yàn què ān zhī hóng hú zhī zhì

how can the sparrow know the great swan's ambitions

Small-minded people cannot understand the great aspirations of those with vision.

The sparrow lives in the hedge and completes its hedge-world with full competence and never wonders about the sky routes of the migrating birds. What the great bird knows of wind and distance is simply outside the vocabulary of the sparrow's concerns. This is not contempt but recognition: not every perspective can hold every kind of ambition.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Shi Ji 史記·陳涉世家 (Chén Shè biography)

Read the full reading
134

不bù打dǎ無wú準zhǔn備bèi之zhī仗zhàng

bù dǎ wú zhǔn bèi zhī zhàng

do not fight battles without preparation

Never enter a conflict or undertaking without thorough preparation.

The battle entered without preparation is not a battle but a donation. What is given freely and without resistance to the opponent who did prepare is not courage but its absence. Readiness is not paranoia; it is the respect that the difficulty ahead deserves, and giving it that respect is the first act of taking it seriously.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 金 Metal 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese military saying

Read the full reading
135

見jiàn義yì思sī義yì，見jiàn危wēi授shòu命mìng

jiàn yì sī yì jiàn wēi shòu mìng

see righteousness, think of righteousness; see danger, give your life

When you see what is right, act on it; when you see danger, be willing to sacrifice for it.

The full expression of commitment is only visible when tested at the extreme, and the test of extreme danger is the final examination of whether the values were genuinely held or only comfortably professed. Those who act in the crisis reveal not what they decided in that moment but what they had been deciding all along, in quieter moments that now stand confirmed. The character is consistent; only the visibility changes.

諺語 Proverb Courage & Decisive Action Present-minded 火 Fire 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·憲問 (Xiàn Wèn XIV)

Read the full reading
136

家jiā有yǒu黃huáng金jīn，不bù如rú有yǒu個gè好hǎo鄰lín居jū

jiā yǒu huáng jīn bù rú yǒu gè hǎo lín jū

home has gold, not equal to having a good neighbor

A good neighbor is worth more than household gold.

Gold cannot run next door in an emergency. The good neighbor is wealth that is immediately accessible, already warm to your situation, and willing to respond at midnight without being asked twice. All the gold in the house cannot tell whether you are well; the good neighbor can and will. This is a different kind of richness.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
137

一yī方fāng水shuǐ土tǔ養yǎng一yī方fāng人rén

yī fāng shuǐ tǔ yǎng yī fāng rén

one region water and soil nurtures one region people

The land and water of a region shapes the people who live there.

The river that runs through the valley for ten thousand years has entered the bones of everyone who drank from it. People are, in part, an expression of the particular earth and water that formed them, and what looks like character is often also geography. Know where someone is from and you begin to understand the soil they grew in.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 水 Water 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
138

安ān土tǔ重zhòng遷qiān

ān tǔ zhòng qiān

at peace with soil, consider moving seriously

People are attached to their homeland and reluctant to leave; the weight of home.

The soil of one's birth is not just earth but memory pressed into ground, and the foot that knows it does not lift easily. The reluctance to move is not weakness but depth: the recognition that what was built here took years and cannot be packed. Roots are not chains but choices that have grown heavy with time.

成語 Adage Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Han Shu 漢書·元帝紀 (Yuán Dì Jì)

Read the full reading
139

父fù母mǔ在zài，不bù遠yuǎn遊yóu

fù mǔ zài, bù yuǎn yóu

while the parents live, do not travel far

Stay near enough to care for aging parents while you still can.

There is a phone call you keep meaning to make longer, a visit you keep folding into next season. The distance you are keeping feels like freedom and it is partly fear. The window for being near them is not wide, and it only closes from one side.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Analects 論語, Book 4

Read the full reading
140

兄xiōng弟dì齊qí心xīn，其qí利lì斷duàn金jīn

xiōng dì qí xīn qí lì duàn jīn

brothers unified in heart, their sharpness cuts metal

When family members stand together with one mind, they can overcome any obstacle.

No single blade is sharper than two that are aligned. The same force distributed across a unified edge cuts through what one blade alone cannot begin. Family that stands together multiplies what each one carries alone.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source I Ching 易經, Xi Ci 繫辭 commentary

Read the full reading
141

前qián人rén種zhòng樹shù，後hòu人rén乘chéng涼liáng

qián rén zhòng shù hòu rén chéng liáng

former people plant trees, later people enjoy cool shade

Those who come before plant trees; those who come after enjoy the shade — the value of contributing to what outlasts you.

The old man who plants a tree he will never sit under has understood something about time that most people spend their whole lives arguing against. Generosity extended forward into years you will not see is the longest form of love. What is planted in one life becomes shelter in another.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Looks ahead 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb; recorded in Qing dynasty collections

Read the full reading
142

千qiān里lǐ姻yīn緣yuán一yī線xiàn牽qiān

qiān lǐ yīn yuán yī xiàn qiān

thousand li marriage fate one thread pulls

Lovers destined for each other are connected by an invisible thread across thousands of miles.

The red thread is tied before the meeting, by an old man at the moon who reads the book of fated unions. Two people whose names share a page do not need to understand the geography; the thread does the navigating. Destiny is not explanation — it is the feeling of having arrived where you were always going.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 火 Fire 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Tang Dynasty 唐·李復言《續玄怪錄》 (Xù Xuán Guài Lù, Story of the Old Man of the Moon)

Read the full reading
143

家jiā和hé萬wàn事shì興xīng

jiā hé wàn shì xīng

family harmonious, ten thousand affairs flourish

When the household is at peace, everything else succeeds.

Fix the house and the world grows quieter. When the people under one roof are at peace, the ten thousand small troubles outside lose their teeth.

俗語 Saying Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source folk proverb; Qing-novel phrasing

Read the full reading
144

慈cí母mǔ多duō敗bài子zǐ

cí mǔ duō bài zǐ

overindulgent mother, many failed children

Excessive parental indulgence often produces spoiled children; love without discipline causes harm.

The love that cannot bring itself to say no is not deficient in love but in the direction love can sometimes point. The child who never meets a boundary never develops the internal structure that eventually becomes their own self-governance. True nurturing asks as much as it provides, and the asking is part of what it provides.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (yanyu)

Read the full reading
145

有yǒu緣yuán千qiān里lǐ來lái相xiāng會huì，無wú緣yuán對duì面miàn不bù相xiāng逢féng

yǒu yuán qiān lǐ lái xiāng huì wú yuán duì miàn bù xiāng féng

with fate, a thousand li come to meet; without fate, face to face and still no meeting

Those with a destined connection will find each other across any distance; those without will miss each other even standing side by side.

The thousand li are not the difficulty; they are just the background across which fate moves two people toward each other. The more remarkable thing is the second half: people standing close enough to touch who do not truly meet because the moment has not been prepared. Distance is not the variable. The connection is the variable, and when it is present, distance dissolves into logistics.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese proverb (appears in various forms in Ming and Qing literature)

Read the full reading
146

源yuán遠yuǎn流liú長cháng

yuán yuǎn liú cháng

source distant, current long

A distant source sustains a long river; a long heritage ensures lasting influence.

The river that has been running for centuries carries in it the rain of a hundred mountain winters and ten thousand springs. What comes from far back has been filtered and shaped by everything it has passed through. Depth of origin is what gives a thing its staying power in the world.

成語 Adage Home, Family & Roots Looks back 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Tang Dynasty 唐·韓愈《原道》 (Yuán Dào, Han Yu essay)

Read the full reading
147

龍lóng生shēng龍lóng，鳳fèng生shēng鳳fèng

lóng shēng lóng fèng shēng fèng

dragon begets dragon, phoenix begets phoenix

Like begets like; children inherit the nature and qualities of their parents.

The dragon's child does not emerge as a sparrow; what is seeded in one generation rises in the next in recognizable form. This is not merely genetics but pattern, the way a way of moving through the world transmits itself through the quality of attention the young receive from the old. The fire passes forward if it is tended.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 火 Fire 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
148

一yī見jiàn鍾zhōng情qíng

yī jiàn zhōng qíng

one glance, love determined

Love at first sight; immediate and complete falling in love.

The recognition that arrives without reasoning is not irrational but pre-rational, a knowing that precedes the explanation. The heart that has already decided before the mind has had a chance to deliberate is not making an error; it is using a different instrument. Whether that instrument proves accurate is what the rest of the story discovers.

成語 Adage Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 火 Fire 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Traditional Chinese folk expression (common in literary and folk culture)

Read the full reading
149

家jiā醜chǒu不bù可kě外wài揚yáng

jiā chǒu bù kě wài yáng

family shames must not be spread outside

Keep the household's internal troubles within the household.

You are tempted to hand your family's tender wound to someone who will only use it as a story. Some things heal in the dark of the house and rot in the open air of gossip. Guard the door, not out of pride, but because the people inside are still yours to protect.

俗語 Saying Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source 五燈會元

Read the full reading
150

兒ér行xíng千qiān里lǐ母mǔ擔dān憂yōu

ér xíng qiān lǐ mǔ dān yōu

when a child travels a thousand li, the mother worries

A parent's love reaches across any distance the child roams.

However far you have gone to build your own life, there is someone counting the miles in the other direction. You may have stopped needing them to worry, but the worrying never asked your permission and never stops. Let them fret; it is the last long rope still tying you home.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Looks back 土 Earth 羊 Year of the Goat

Source 增廣賢文

Read the full reading
151

愛ài之zhī欲yù深shēn，則zé知zhī其qí失shī

ài zhī yù shēn zé zhī qí shī

love deeply then know the loss

The deeper the love, the more keenly felt the loss; deep attachment brings deep grief.

What we hold most tightly we feel most acutely when the hand is empty. Love is not diminished by this truth but described by it, since only what mattered deeply can leave so large a space. The grief is the measure of what was there, and that is not a small thing.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 火 Fire 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Han Yu 韓愈·《祭十二郎文》 (Jì Shí'èr Láng Wén, Tang Dynasty memorial essay)

Read the full reading
152

春chūn草cǎo年nián年nián綠lǜ，王wáng孫sūn歸guī不bù歸guī

chūn cǎo nián nián lǜ wáng sūn guī bù guī

spring grass year after year green, wandering son return or not return

Nature renews itself endlessly, but the wanderer may or may not return — longing for the absent.

The grass does not wait for the traveler; it simply keeps being green in the spring, reliably, as if demonstrating what faithfulness without expectation looks like. The one who went away is thought of in terms of this grass: will they return when it is green again. The seasons mark the absences.

詩詞 Verse Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Wang Wei 王維·《山中送別》 (Shān Zhōng Sòng Bié, Tang Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
153

落luò葉yè歸guī根gēn

luò yè guī gēn

fallen leaves return to the roots

All things are drawn back toward their origin and their home.

However far the wind takes a leaf, the root is where it is going. There is no shame in the turn toward home; it is the shape the whole year was making.

成語 Adage Home, Family & Roots Looks back 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source 景德傳燈錄 (Song Buddhist text)

Read the full reading
154

愛ài屋wū及jí烏wū

ài wū jí wū

love the house extend to the crow on it

Love me, love my dog; extend affection for something to everything associated with it.

The crow on the roof of the beloved house becomes beautiful by association, transformed not by its own qualities but by what surrounds it. Love has this generous property: it cannot keep itself confined to its exact object and spills over onto everything adjacent. This is both its beauty and its danger.

成語 Adage Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 火 Fire 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Tai Gong Bing Fa 太公兵法 (Tài Gōng Bīng Fǎ, attributed to Jiang Ziya)

Read the full reading
155

一yī人rén得dé道dào，雞jī犬quǎn升shēng天tiān

yī rén dé dào jī quǎn shēng tiān

one person attains the way, chickens and dogs ascend to heaven

When one person rises, all those connected to them benefit; nepotism or reflected glory.

The ascension of one can lift the whole household, for good or ill, depending on how the rising was earned. Connected fortune is real but it is borrowed, and borrowed fortune must be returned when the connection changes. Build your own ladder.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 火 Fire 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Wang Chong 王充·《論衡》 (Lùn Héng, Weighing Discourses, Han Dynasty)

Read the full reading
156

家jiā有yǒu一yī老lǎo，如rú有yǒu一yī寶bǎo

jiā yǒu yī lǎo rú yǒu yī bǎo

a household with one elder is like having one treasure

An elderly person in the family is a priceless treasure of wisdom and experience.

The elder does not give you information — they give you pattern recognition across a longer span of time than you have lived. What looks like a crisis to you is a version of something they have already watched resolve. That perspective is worth more than any object in the house.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Chinese folk proverb 民間諺語

Read the full reading
157

本běn是shì同tóng根gēn生shēng，相xiāng煎jiān何hé太tài急jí

běn shì tóng gēn shēng xiāng jiān hé tài jí

originally from the same root grown, why cook each other so urgently

Those of the same origin should not harm each other; the cruelty of turning on one's own kin.

The poet's brother ordered him to write in seven paces or face death, and he walked and wrote of beans boiling under fire from their own stalks. They grew from the same root, and the stalk is burning the seed of itself. Some betrayals are the most particular kind of cruelty: the violence of the familiar.

詩詞 Verse Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 木 Wood 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Cao Zhi 曹植·《七步詩》 (Qī Bù Shī, Seven Steps Poem, Three Kingdoms period)

Read the full reading
158

狗gǒu不bù嫌xián家jiā貧pín

gǒu bù xián jiā pín

a dog does not scorn a poor home

Loyalty does not weigh a home by its wealth; it stays for love rather than for gain.

You do not love people only in their good seasons. When a home grows lean or a friend grows hard to reach, the part of you that stays looks like foolishness from far away and reads, up close, as the steadiest loyalty there is.

俗語 Saying Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source folk proverb; paired with 子不嫌母醜

Read the full reading
159

獨dú在zài異yì鄉xiāng為wéi異yì客kè，每měi逢féng佳jiā節jié倍bèi思sī親qīn

dú zài yì xiāng wéi yì kè měi féng jiā jié bèi sī qīn

alone in a strange land as a strange guest, every festival doubly think of kin

Far from home, every holiday makes one miss family twice as much.

The festival arrives with its smells and sounds, and for the one who is far away it is precisely these that open the distance rather than close it. Celebration in exile is also a form of grief, the particular ache of knowing what is happening somewhere else that you are not part of. Missing is love at a distance, doing what it can.

詩詞 Verse Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Wang Wei 王維·《九月九日憶山東兄弟》 (Jiǔ Yuè Jiǔ Rì Yì Shāndōng Xiōngdì, Tang Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
160

百bǎi善shàn孝xiào為wéi先xiān

bǎi shàn xiào wéi xiān

of the hundred virtues, filial care comes first

Respect and care for one's parents is the root virtue from which the others grow.

You measure yourself by the good you do out in the world, where it is seen and thanked. But the first ledger was opened long before that, in the house that fed you when you could give nothing back. Settle that account gently, and the rest of your goodness stands on real ground.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Looks back 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source 圍爐夜話

Read the full reading
161

如rú魚yú得dé水shuǐ

rú yú dé shuǐ

like fish getting water

Like a fish in water; feeling completely at home or perfectly suited to a situation.

The fish does not think about the water and the water does not think about the fish; they have achieved the highest kind of fit, the kind that erases the question of fitting. To find the environment where your nature is not a problem but a form is one of the great good fortunes of a life.

成語 Adage Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source San Guo Zhi 三國志·蜀志·諸葛亮傳 (Zhūgě Liàng biography)

Read the full reading
162

家jiā和hé事shì事shì順shùn

jiā hé shì shì shùn

family harmonious, all matters flow smoothly

When the family is in harmony, everything else goes smoothly too.

The ship that is not fighting against itself sails faster in any direction it chooses. Internal harmony is not the absence of complexity but the presence of a fundamental agreement among the parts about what they are together. When the home is right with itself, the world outside becomes navigable in a way it simply is not otherwise.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common household blessing)

Read the full reading
163

民mín以yǐ食shí為wéi天tiān

mín yǐ shí wéi tiān

people take food as heaven

Food is the most basic and essential need of the people; never neglect material necessities.

Before philosophy and after it, the body asks the same question: what will it eat today. The most elegant governance in the world means nothing to an empty stomach, and the wisest counsel has no audience in the face of hunger. Begin with the grain, and the rest becomes possible.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Han Shu 漢書·酈食其傳 (Lì Shíjī biography)

Read the full reading
164

金jīn山shān銀yín山shān，不bù如rú兒ér孫sūn滿mǎn山shān

jīn shān yín shān bù rú ér sūn mǎn shān

mountain of gold, mountain of silver, not equal to children and grandchildren filling the mountain

No amount of wealth compares to a thriving family and descendants.

The gold piled high goes cold in an empty house, and silver accumulated for no one in particular is just weight. What warms the family home is not the account but the voices in the rooms, the feet on the stairs, the names called across the courtyard. The living inheritance is the only one that matters.

諺語 Proverb Home, Family & Roots Present-minded 土 Earth 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
165

飲yǐn水shuǐ思sī源yuán

yǐn shuǐ sī yuán

drink water, think of source

When you drink water, remember where it comes from; never forget those who gave you your foundation.

The water that reaches your hand began somewhere in the dark, in the root, in the patience of a hillside after rain. To drink without remembering that origin is to receive a gift without meeting the giver.

成語 Adage Home, Family & Roots Looks back 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Yu Xin 庾信 (Northern Zhou)

Read the full reading
166

坐zuò井jǐng觀guān天tiān

zuò jǐng guān tiān

sit in well, observe sky

Have a limited view; the narrow perspective of someone who has not ventured beyond their small world.

The frog at the bottom of the well is not wrong about the sky — it is just that the circle of sky it sees is genuinely all the sky it knows. The limitation is not an error in observation but in geography. All perspective is partial; the wise one at least knows where their well ends.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water Year of the Frog

Source Zhuangzi 莊子·秋水 (Qiū Shuǐ, Autumn Floods)

Read the full reading
167

寸cùn進jìn尺chǐ退tuì

cùn jìn chǐ tuì

inch forward, foot back

Gain an inch and lose a foot; small gains don't compensate for larger losses.

Some arrangements look like progress because something moved forward, and the thing that moved backward was not measured. The wise keep accounts of both columns, the gained and the given away, because the net sum is the only true number. Partial scorecards lead to confident losers.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Zhanguo Ce 戰國策 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Strategies of the Warring States)

Read the full reading
168

早zǎo知zhī今jīn日rì，何hé必bì當dāng初chū

zǎo zhī jīn rì hé bì dāng chū

had I known today, why would I have done what I did at the start

Had I known how it would turn out, I would have acted differently; hindsight brings regret.

The clarity of hindsight carries the peculiar sadness of its own uselessness: it arrives only after the moment of its application has passed. This regret is the tax we pay for not having been wiser in the moment, and the only direction it can be redirected is forward, as a sharpened attention that watches the next beginning more carefully than the last.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Looks back 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common in folk and literary contexts)

Read the full reading
169

水shuǐ能néng載zài舟zhōu亦yì能néng覆fù舟zhōu

shuǐ néng zài zhōu yì néng fù zhōu

water can carry the boat, and can also capsize the boat

The people can support a ruler or overthrow one; the same force that sustains can destroy.

The river does not change its nature depending on who asks it to carry them. Water supports what is placed on it correctly and unmakes what sits on it wrongly. The wise ruler who understood this — Tang Taizong of the Tang Dynasty, inheriting the wisdom — kept the people as the first consideration not from sentiment but from the clearest political perception: the force beneath you is the same force that could end you. Earn it.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Tang Taizong 唐太宗 (attributed, from Zhen Guan Zheng Yao 貞觀政要, compiled by Wu Jing)

Read the full reading
170

腦nǎo中zhōng自zì有yǒu黃huáng金jīn屋wū

nǎo zhōng zì yǒu huáng jīn wū

within the mind naturally resides a golden house

Knowledge is the greatest treasure; the mind holds a palace.

The house of gold in the mind does not rust and cannot be stolen. Every skill, every book absorbed, every lesson taken to heart adds another chamber to a dwelling that expands without limit and that no circumstance of the external world can repossess. The most portable form of wealth is what the mind has learned to hold.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Song Dynasty 宋·趙恆《勸學詩》 (Quàn Xué Shī, Emperor Zhenzong poem)

Read the full reading
171

舊jiù瓶píng裝zhuāng新xīn酒jiǔ

jiù píng zhuāng xīn jiǔ

old bottle contains new wine

Old forms containing new content; using familiar structures for fresh ideas.

The bottle does not argue with what it is asked to hold, and the wine does not require a new container to be itself. Sometimes the old form is the wisest vessel for what is genuinely new, providing a structure that people trust while the contents surprise them. Not everything needs a new jar to matter.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese proverb based on biblical parallel; common in Chinese folk usage

Read the full reading
172

黑hēi髮fà不bù知zhī勤qín學xué早zǎo

hēi fà bù zhī qín xué zǎo

black hair does not know to study diligently early

Youth does not realize the importance of studying early; one regrets wasted youth.

The young body has not yet felt the weight of time and so does not believe in it. Only looking back does one see the open field of hours that was available and is now behind the fence. This regret, once understood, can be given as a gift forward to those still standing in the field.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Looks back 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Yan Zhenqing 顏真卿·《勸學詩》 (Quàn Xué Shī, Poem Encouraging Learning, Tang Dynasty)

Read the full reading
173

鼠shǔ目mù寸cùn光guāng

shǔ mù cùn guāng

rat eyes, inch of light

Short-sighted; lacking vision or foresight; able to see only what is immediately in front.

The mouse's eye is perfectly calibrated for the inch of world the mouse inhabits. For the mouse, this is not a limitation but a perfect match between instrument and requirement. For the person who is also capable of longer vision, to use only the mouse's inch is the waste of an unused gift. The eye that can see the horizon should use it.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in folk and literary contexts)

Read the full reading
174

不bù恥chǐ下xià問wèn

bù chǐ xià wèn

not ashamed to ask those below

A person of true learning is not embarrassed to seek knowledge from those of lesser rank or experience.

Pride costs more than ignorance. The person who cannot ask a junior colleague, a young student, a stranger on the road — that person stops learning the moment they stop being the youngest person in the room. Knowledge does not care about rank. It goes to whoever is willing to receive it.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Analects 論語, Book 5 (Gongye Chang 公冶長)

Read the full reading
175

十shí年nián樹shù木mù，百bǎi年nián樹shù人rén

shí nián shù mù bǎi nián shù rén

ten years to grow a tree, hundred years to cultivate a person

It takes a century to properly raise and educate a person; education is a long-term investment.

The oak planted today will outlive the planter, and the lesson given today will outlive the lesson-giver. To invest in a person is to cast a shadow forward through time further than any single life can reach. This is the longest form of building.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 木 Wood 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Guanzi 管子·權修 (Quán Xiū chapter)

Read the full reading
176

實shí事shì求qiú是shì

shí shì qiú shì

actual matters seek truth

Seek truth from facts; be realistic and practical.

The map must not be more beautiful than the territory it describes. What is true has a texture, a resistance to wishful redrawing, and the person who keeps returning to the actual thing rather than the comfortable idea of it lives in a more reliable world. Truth is not cold; it is simply honest about the weather.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Han Shu 漢書·河間獻王傳 (Hé Jiān Xiàn Wáng biography)

Read the full reading
177

循xún循xún善shàn誘yòu

xún xún shàn yòu

step-by-step, well-guided

Guide patiently and skillfully step by step; good teaching through gentle, systematic encouragement.

The patient teacher does not push from behind but moves just ahead, visible and unhurrying, offering the next step only when the current one is steady. What is drawn forward by interest lasts longer than what is pushed by pressure, and the learner led by curiosity rather than fear arrives somewhere quite different from the one who arrived in a hurry.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·子罕 (Zǐ Hǎn IX)

Read the full reading
178

溫wēn故gù知zhī新xīn

wēn gù zhī xīn

warm the old, know the new

Reviewing what you have learned yields fresh understanding.

The old ground still has new things in it. Turn over what you already know with fresh attention and it yields a harvest you missed the first time.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Looks back 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Analects 論語, Book 2

Read the full reading
179

知zhī之zhī為wéi知zhī之zhī，不bù知zhī為wéi不bù知zhī

zhī zhī wéi zhī zhī bù zhī wéi bù zhī

know it then say know it, not know then say not know

Acknowledge what you know and admit what you don't; intellectual honesty is true wisdom.

The admission of not knowing is not a diminishment but a clearing, a making of space for what can be learned. Pretending to know what you do not is a tax levied against future understanding: you must maintain the pretense, which costs energy that would have been better spent learning. Say you do not know, and you have already begun.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·為政 (Wèi Zhèng II)

Read the full reading
180

聞wén道dào有yǒu先xiān後hòu，術shù業yè有yǒu專zhuān攻gōng

wén dào yǒu xiān hòu shù yè yǒu zhuān gōng

hearing the Way has earlier and later, skill and profession have specialization

Some learn the Way before others; everyone has their area of expertise; respect both seniority in learning and specialization.

The carpenter knows things about wood that the scholar does not, and the scholar knows things about texts that the carpenter has not needed to know. There is no hierarchy between these wisdoms, only the recognition that knowing arrives in different orders for different people, and that expertise lives in particular domains, not in persons as undivided wholes.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Han Yu 韓愈·《師說》 (Shī Shuō, On Being a Teacher, Tang Dynasty essay)

Read the full reading
181

身shēn教jiào不bù言yán而ér從cóng

shēn jiào bù yán ér cóng

body teaching without speaking still followed

Teaching through example, without words, is more powerful than verbal instruction.

The child who watches how the parent moves through a difficulty learns more in those moments than in any lecture about how to move through a difficulty. The body in action is the truest text, and those who teach by living their values create followers who have understood at the level where understanding sticks.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese educational philosophy

Read the full reading
182

狐hú假jiǎ虎hǔ威wēi

hú jiǎ hǔ wēi

fox borrows tiger's authority

Using another's power to bully or impress; claiming borrowed authority.

The fox with no claws of its own walks in the shadow of the tiger and lets others mistake the shadow for substance. Borrowed power is a costume, and the moment its source departs, so does the authority. Those who build nothing of their own are always one step from nakedness.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Warring States 戰國策·楚策一 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Chu Strategies I)

Read the full reading
183

不bù識shí廬lú山shān真zhēn面miàn目mù

bù shí lú shān zhēn miàn mù

not recognize Mount Lu's true face

Unable to see the true nature of something because you are too close to it.

The person standing inside the mountain cannot see its shape. Only distance permits the view, which is why outsiders sometimes understand what insiders cannot, and why time is the greatest cartographer. Step back, and the mountain introduces itself.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Su Shi 蘇軾·《題西林壁》 (Tí Xī Lín Bì, Song Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
184

井jǐng底dǐ之zhī蛙wā，不bù可kě以yǐ語yǔ於yú海hǎi

jǐng dǐ zhī wā bù kě yǐ yǔ yú hǎi

frog of well bottom cannot be spoken to about the sea

You cannot discuss vast matters with someone of limited experience; the frog in the well cannot comprehend the ocean.

The frog of the well has not been dishonest about its knowledge; it simply has not had the experience of the sea. This is not stupidity but geography. Before judging the comprehension, consider the size of the world the other person has had access to. Expand the world before you expand the expectation.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Zhuangzi 莊子·秋水 (Qiū Shuǐ, Autumn Floods)

Read the full reading
185

因yīn人rén而ér異yì

yīn rén ér yì

because of person, therefore different

Varies according to the individual; treat each person differently as the situation requires.

The teacher who teaches every student identically is not teaching but broadcasting into an empty room. Each person learns by a different door, and the one who finds that door is not favoring the student but serving them. The instruction that fits becomes the instruction that enters.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese idiom in educational philosophy

Read the full reading
186

因yīn地dì制zhì宜yí

yīn dì zhì yí

according to place, adapt to the appropriate

Adapt measures to local conditions; tailor approaches to the specific situation.

The same seed planted in different soils does not grow identically, because the conversation between seed and soil produces what is appropriate to the specific meeting. What works in the valley may not work on the hillside, and the wisdom is not in the method but in the reading of where the method is being applied. Place teaches.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese agricultural and strategic principle

Read the full reading
187

學xué然rán後hòu知zhī不bù足zú

xué rán hòu zhī bù zú

study then know insufficiency

Only after studying do you realize how much you still lack; learning reveals ignorance.

The great paradox of study: the more you learn, the larger the unknown becomes. The beginner's ignorance is simple; the advanced student's ignorance is mapped and intricate, a more detailed picture of what remains. This is progress — not the elimination of the unknown but its clarification and expansion. The wise student is always discovering new ways they need to learn more.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Li Ji 禮記·學記 (Xué Jì, Record of Learning)

Read the full reading
188

明míng哲zhé保bǎo身shēn

míng zhé bǎo shēn

bright wisdom protects the self

The wise person recognizes danger and keeps safe; prudent self-preservation.

Knowing when to step forward and when to be still is the older half of courage. The candle that understands the wind does not need to be brave, only observant. Wisdom sometimes looks like withdrawal and is actually survival.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Shi Jing 詩經·大雅·烝民 (Zhēng Mín, Book of Songs)

Read the full reading
189

水shuǐ中zhōng撈lāo月yuè一yī場chǎng空kōng

shuǐ zhōng lāo yuè yī chǎng kōng

fishing moon from water, one whole emptiness

Futile effort to grasp what cannot be caught; chasing an impossible illusion.

The moon in the water is real as an image and non-existent as a thing to hold. The hand reaches in and closes on the impression of moonlight and nothing more. To know the difference between the image of what you want and the actual thing requires a clarity that desire does not naturally provide. Look up; the moon is already where it is.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese folk story (common Zen-Buddhist and Taoist metaphor)

Read the full reading
190

瓜guā田tián李lǐ下xià

guā tián lǐ xià

melon field, plum tree below

Avoid places or situations where your conduct might be misinterpreted; keep clear of suspicion.

Adjusting your shoe in a melon field looks like stealing even when it is not. The appearance of doing wrong is not the same as doing wrong, but in the world of other people's watching, the difference is academic. Wisdom sometimes means choosing a different place to tie your sandal.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Wei Dynasty 魏·曹植《君子行》 (Jūn Zǐ Xíng)

Read the full reading
191

百bǎi聞wén不bù如rú一yī見jiàn

bǎi wén bù rú yī jiàn

hundred hearings not equal one seeing

Seeing once is worth more than hearing a hundred times.

A thousand descriptions of the sea cannot summon the smell of salt or the cold shock of a wave against the chest. The body learns what words can only approximate, and the eye confirms what the ear has long held as rumor. Go and look.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Han Shu 漢書·趙充國傳 (Zhào Chōng Guó biography)

Read the full reading
192

能néng問wèn能néng悟wù，能néng悟wù能néng成chéng

néng wèn néng wù néng wù néng chéng

can ask can understand, can understand can achieve

The ability to ask leads to understanding; understanding leads to achievement.

The question is the opening and the understanding is what enters through it, and from understanding the doing becomes possible in a way it was not before. Each step requires the previous one, and the first step — the willingness to ask, to not know, to open — is the one most often skipped by pride. Ask. Understand. Do. In that order.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese educational proverb

Read the full reading
193

博bó學xué篤dǔ志zhì

bó xué dǔ zhì

broad learning, steadfast aspiration

Study broadly and hold to one's purpose firmly; wide knowledge with focused intention.

The tree with wide roots and a single trunk upright: this is the form of a mind that reads everything and still knows what it is about. Breadth without direction becomes noise, and direction without breadth becomes narrowness. The combination is what produces someone the world actually needs to hear from.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·子張 (Zǐ Zhāng XIX)

Read the full reading
194

學xué無wú止zhǐ境jìng

xué wú zhǐ jìng

learning has no stopping point

Learning has no end; there is always more to know and understand.

The one who says they have finished learning has confused a plateau with a summit. Every answer opens a corridor of further questions, and the corridor has no back wall. This is not exhausting but orienting: the direction is always forward, and the life spent in learning is always, by definition, unfinished and therefore alive.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese saying (common in educational and philosophical contexts)

Read the full reading
195

欲yù窮qióng千qiān里lǐ目mù，更gèng上shàng一yī層céng樓lóu

yù qióng qiān lǐ mù gèng shàng yī céng lóu

wish to see a thousand li, climb one more floor

To see further, you must rise higher; if you want greater understanding, strive upward.

The view from the penultimate floor is already extraordinary, but the next floor contains a horizon the current floor has hidden. Growth does not arrive as a conclusion but as a view that reveals what was always just beyond the previous vantage. There is always one more floor, and it always pays to climb it.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 火 Fire 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Wang Zhihuan 王之渙·《登鸛雀樓》 (Dēng Guàn Què Lóu, Tang Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
196

殊shū途tú同tóng歸guī

shū tú tóng guī

different routes, same destination

Different paths lead to the same end; various approaches can arrive at the same conclusion.

The mountain has as many paths to its peak as there are people who have climbed it, and the summit is the same summit regardless of which face was chosen. The road that works for one person does not need to be the road for every person; what matters is that the road leads where it claims to lead. Different does not mean wrong.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Yi Jing 易經·繫辭下 (Xì Cí II)

Read the full reading
197

無wú風fēng不bù起qǐ浪làng

wú fēng bù qǐ làng

no wind does not raise waves

There is no smoke without fire; every effect has a cause.

The water was calm and then it was not, and the disturbance knows what made it. Rumors, rumors especially, are never entirely without foundation even when they are entirely without fairness. Look for the wind before you dismiss the waves.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
198

知zhī行xíng合hé一yī

zhī xíng hé yī

knowledge and action merge into one

Knowing and doing are one; understanding is not complete until it is expressed in action.

The knowledge that remains purely abstract is not yet fully itself; it is still waiting for the action that will complete it. Wang Yangming's great insight was that these two things — knowing and doing — are not sequential but simultaneous: the knowing is already in the beginning of the doing, and the doing is what the knowing always was leading to. They are one thing divided for examination.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 火 Fire 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Wang Yangming 王陽明 (Ming Dynasty Neo-Confucian philosopher)

Read the full reading
199

青qīng出chū於yú藍lán而ér勝shèng於yú藍lán

qīng chū yú lán ér shèng yú lán

blue comes from indigo yet surpasses indigo

The student surpasses the teacher; the new generation exceeds the old.

The indigo plant does not resent the deeper blue it produces; it is fulfilled by it. The teacher who raises a student beyond their own ceiling has completed their purpose and should feel the particular satisfaction of having been surpassed. This is how knowledge grows past any single vessel.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Xun Zi 荀子·勸學 (Quàn Xué, Encouraging Learning)

Read the full reading
200

高gāo瞻zhān遠yuǎn矚zhǔ

gāo zhān yuǎn zhǔ

look high, gaze far

Think ahead with broad vision; farsighted strategic thinking.

The crane standing on the rock at the edge of the marsh sees the storm arriving long before those who are sheltered in the reeds. Height of perspective is not arrogance but a gift, one that carries the obligation to speak clearly of what is seen. The view from above is only useful when it comes back down.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in political and strategic literature)

Read the full reading
201

一yī字zì千qiān金jīn

yī zì qiān jīn

one character worth a thousand gold

Every word is precious; writing of supreme value.

The right word at the right moment is not decoration but architecture — it holds the whole thought upright. One phrase placed exactly can outweigh a season of noise. Write less; mean more.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Shi Ji 史記·呂不韋列傳 (Lǚ Bùwéi biography)

Read the full reading
202

一yī目mù瞭liǎo然rán

yī mù liǎo rán

one glance, completely clear

Understand at a single glance; immediately and perfectly clear.

Some things do not require explanation because they are organized the way truth is organized: so clearly that the eye and the mind arrive at the same conclusion in the same instant. Clarity is not simplicity but honesty of structure. What can be understood in one look was built with care.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 火 Fire 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in classical and modern contexts)

Read the full reading
203

不bù經jīng一yī事shì，不bù長zhǎng一yī智zhì

bù jīng yī shì bù zhǎng yī zhì

not pass through one matter, not grow one wisdom

Without experience, there is no growth in wisdom.

Every ordeal is a private tutor arriving uninvited. The lesson would be refused if it came politely announced, so it comes as trouble. Afterward, if you are paying attention, you find you know something you did not know before and cannot say when the teaching happened.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Looks back 火 Fire 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Qing Dynasty 清·曹雪芹《紅樓夢》 (Hóng Lóu Mèng, Dream of the Red Chamber)

Read the full reading
204

人rén之zhī將jiāng死sǐ，其qí言yán也yě善shàn

rén zhī jiāng sǐ qí yán yě shàn

when a person is about to die, their words are also good

A dying person's words are sincere and good; truth spoken at the end of life carries special weight.

The person near the end has no more time for strategy or performance. What is said at the final threshold is what was always most true, now arriving without the usual filters. This is why the last words carry their particular weight: they are the person finally speaking without the life they were constructing to protect. The end is the most honest hour.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·泰伯 (Tài Bó VIII)

Read the full reading
205

以yǐ德dé報bào怨yuàn，何hé以yǐ報bào德dé

yǐ dé bào yuàn hé yǐ bào dé

repay grievance with virtue, then what to repay virtue with

If you repay injury with virtue, what do you have left with which to repay genuine kindness?

The one who uses their finest response on the enemy has spent it; when the friend arrives, only equal amounts remain available for all parties. Confucius argued for proportionality: repay injury with justice, and save virtue for virtue. The hierarchy of response is itself a statement about the hierarchy of relationships.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·憲問 (Xiàn Wèn XIV)

Read the full reading
206

學xué海hǎi無wú涯yá，勤qín為wéi徑jìng

xué hǎi wú yá qín wéi jìng

ocean of learning has no shore, diligence is the path

The ocean of knowledge is boundless; diligence is the way to navigate it.

The sea of learning has no far shore, which means the only vessel that makes sense is one equipped for a permanent voyage rather than a destination. Diligence is not the engine of arrival but the engine of the ongoing motion. The ship that stays seaworthy keeps discovering new water, and this is the whole point.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Han Yu 韓愈·《進學解》 (Jìn Xué Jiě, Explanation on Advancing in Learning)

Read the full reading
207

書shū到dào用yòng時shí方fāng恨hèn少shǎo

shū dào yòng shí fāng hèn shǎo

books when needed then regret few

Only when you need knowledge do you realize how little you have stored; learn while you can.

The well seems deep enough until the drought comes. What was read and put aside but not truly held returns as gap at the exact moment a bridge was needed. Study is not preparation for some future occasion; it is the slow building of the bridge before the river is in front of you.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Ming Dynasty 明·諺語 (Ming dynasty folk proverb)

Read the full reading
208

畫huà蛇shé添tiān足zú

huà shé tiān zú

draw snake, add feet

Add something superfluous that ruins the whole; ruin something by over-elaborating.

The snake was perfect, complete, and won the competition, and the painter who added feet to his winning entry did not improve it but disqualified it. The addition that was not needed made something sufficient into something wrong. Knowing when to stop is as important as knowing how to start, and the temptation to add one more thing is the more dangerous instinct.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Warring States 戰國策·齊策二 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Qi Strategies II)

Read the full reading
209

空kōng穴xué來lái風fēng

kōng xué lái fēng

empty cave attracts wind

Rumors don't arise without some basis; there is always some cause behind the talk.

The cave that draws wind does so because it is open, and the opening is always there before the wind arrives. A story gains traction because it found something in reality to grip. The question is not whether the rumor is exactly true but what in the world gave it its first purchase.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Song Yu 宋玉·《風賦》 (Fēng Fù, Rhapsody on Wind, Chu)

Read the full reading
210

胸xiōng有yǒu成chéng竹zhú

xiōng yǒu chéng zhú

chest contains fully-formed bamboo

Have a well-thought-out plan; confident and well-prepared with a clear mind.

The painter Wen Tong studied bamboo in all its seasons until he could see the whole plant in his mind before the brush touched the paper. The chest that holds a completed bamboo needs no reference in the moment of painting: the bamboo is already there, waiting only for the hand to release it. This is the confidence of the thoroughly prepared.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Su Shi 蘇軾·《文與可畫筼筜谷偃竹記》 (Wén Yǔ Kě Huà Yùn Dāng Gǔ Yǎn Zhú Jì)

Read the full reading
211

行xíng成chéng於yú思sī，毀huǐ於yú隨suí

xíng chéng yú sī huǐ yú suí

action formed through thought, ruined through following

Achievement is built through careful thought; it is ruined by blindly following others.

What is built through genuine thought has a coherence to it that holds; what is assembled by following others' trends without one's own conviction falls apart when the trend changes, having had no original architecture to hold it together. Think before you act, and think for yourself. The act shaped by your own reflection is the one that belongs to you.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Han Yu 韓愈·《進學解》 (Jìn Xué Jiě, Tang Dynasty essay)

Read the full reading
212

讀dú書shū百bǎi遍biàn，其qí義yì自zì見jiàn

dú shū bǎi biàn qí yì zì jiàn

read a book a hundred times, its meaning reveals itself

Read a difficult text repeatedly and its meaning will eventually become clear on its own.

The hundred readings are not the same reading done a hundred times. Each one arrives with a different reader — someone slightly older, slightly changed by what happened between readings — and finds something slightly different on the page. The text has not changed; the reader has. And at some reading — not predictably the hundredth but somewhere in the accumulation — the meaning stands up and becomes plain. Not forced out but arrived at through faithful return.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Wei Lue 魏略 (Records of Wei, quoted in Pei Songzhi's annotations to Sanguozhi 三國志)

Read the full reading
213

進jìn退tuì有yǒu度dù

jìn tuì yǒu dù

advance and retreat have measure

Know when to advance and when to retreat; move with proper judgment and restraint.

The river that knows its banks does not spread itself thin trying to cover all ground; it flows with purpose. Advance and retreat are both movements, and the one who reads the moment chooses the right one. Control of both directions is what gives the capable person their particular authority.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Traditional Chinese military and philosophical saying

Read the full reading
214

隔gé岸àn觀guān火huǒ

gé àn guān huǒ

from the opposite bank watch the fire

Watch from a safe distance while others suffer; stand aside and observe without helping.

The fire looks dramatic from the other bank, and the distance that makes it safe also makes it someone else's problem. There is a calculation in this that looks like strategy but often turns out to be the kind of safety that makes no friends. The shore you are standing on is only dry until the fire learns to cross water.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 火 Fire 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Sun Zi 孫子兵法·三十六計 (Thirty-Six Stratagems)

Read the full reading
215

上shàng行xíng下xià效xiào

shàng xíng xià xiào

above acts, below follows

Those below follow what those above do; leaders set the example that subordinates imitate.

The behavior at the top of the structure is the curriculum for everyone beneath it, whether or not the top is aware of its teaching role. What the leader does casually, the follower assumes to be the standard. This is not a burden but an invitation: the level of the structure is set by what the highest point habitually does.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Han Shu 漢書·武帝紀 (Wǔ Dì Jì, Emperor Wu)

Read the full reading
216

千qiān里lǐ馬mǎ常cháng有yǒu，而ér伯bó樂lè不bù常cháng有yǒu

qiān lǐ mǎ cháng yǒu ér bó lè bù cháng yǒu

thousand-li horses are common, but Bo Le who recognizes them is not

Talented people are more common than those who can recognize and develop talent.

The exceptional horse exists in every generation; what is rare is the eye that recognizes it as such before it has proven itself. The horse cannot demonstrate what it is without the running, and the running is not offered to horses the world has already decided are ordinary. The judge of unproven excellence is the rarer gift. Be that judge.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Han Yu 韓愈·《馬說》 (Mǎ Shuō, On Horses, Tang Dynasty essay)

Read the full reading
217

南nán轅yuán北běi轍zhé

nán yuán běi zhé

south shaft, north tracks

Going in completely the opposite direction from one's goal; methods contrary to purpose.

The carriage heading north to reach the south is not merely inefficient; it is adding to the distance with every step of progress it makes. When the method contradicts the destination, effort becomes its own obstacle. Before the first stride, check which way the road is facing.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Warring States 戰國策·魏策四 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Wei Strategies IV)

Read the full reading
218

書shū山shān有yǒu路lù勤qín為wéi徑jìng

shū shān yǒu lù qín wéi jìng

the mountain of books has a path, diligence is the route

In the mountain of knowledge, the only road is diligence.

The mountain of books is not a metaphor for difficulty but for genuine magnitude: there is more to know than any one life can hold. And the path through it — the only path — is the one made by repeated, consistent, unhurried effort. No shortcut cuts through a mountain. You walk the path by reading one page, then another, returning the next day, and the day after that, for years.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Han Yu 韓愈 (Tang Dynasty couplet on learning, often paired with 學海無涯苦作舟)

Read the full reading
219

老lǎo馬mǎ識shí途tú

lǎo mǎ shí tú

old horse knows the road

An experienced person knows the way; value the wisdom of those with experience.

The old horse has traveled this road in every season and remembers it in its muscles rather than its mind. What experience stores in the body is not data but knowing, the kind that does not need to be recalled because it never left. Defer to the seasoned traveler on questions of path; they have already paid for the information with their years.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Han Feizi 韓非子·說林上 (Shuō Lín I, Forest of Persuasions)

Read the full reading
220

讀dú萬wàn卷juǎn書shū，行xíng萬wàn里lǐ路lù

dú wàn juǎn shū xíng wàn lǐ lù

read ten thousand volumes, travel ten thousand li

Combine book learning with real-world experience; theory and practice together make wisdom.

The scholar who never leaves the library knows all the maps but has felt no rain. The traveler who never reads has felt everything but can name nothing. The fullest life is the conversation between the page and the road, each correcting and completing the other.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Ming Dynasty 明·董其昌《畫禪室隨筆》 (Huà Chán Shì Suí Bǐ, Dong Qichang)

Read the full reading
221

風fēng物wù長cháng宜yí放fàng眼yǎn量liàng

fēng wù cháng yí fàng yǎn liàng

scenery and things long suitable for wide-eye measurement

Take the long view on all things; assess matters from a broad and patient perspective.

The panorama of the long view makes the present irritation smaller and the present comfort less absolute. Both the difficulty and the joy look different when set against the wide horizon that time provides. The eye that can hold the far distance without losing sight of the near is the eye that judges accurately.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 木 Wood 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Mao Zedong 毛澤東·《七律·和柳亞子先生》 (Hé Liǔ Yǎzǐ Xiānsheng, 1949 poem)

Read the full reading
222

三sān人rén行xíng，必bì有yǒu我wǒ師shī

sān rén xíng, bì yǒu wǒ shī

of three walking together, one is my teacher

There is always someone, in any company, from whom you can learn.

You quietly rank the people around you, deciding who is worth listening to before they have spoken. The one you have written off is holding the exact thing you are missing. Walk beside them as a student and the ordinary afternoon starts teaching.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Analects 論語, Book 7

Read the full reading
223

仁rén者zhě見jiàn仁rén，智zhì者zhě見jiàn智zhì

rén zhě jiàn rén zhì zhě jiàn zhì

benevolent sees benevolence, wise sees wisdom

Different people see the same thing differently based on their own nature.

The same mountain in autumn looks like sorrow to one painter and abundance to another, and neither is wrong. Every eye is also a lens ground by the life behind it. Understanding why another person sees what they see is the beginning of real conversation.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Yi Jing 易經·繫辭上 (Xì Cí I)

Read the full reading
224

矛máo盾dùn

máo dùn

spear shield

A contradiction; an irreconcilable conflict.

The spear that can pierce anything and the shield that nothing can pierce cannot exist in the same world, and the merchant who sold both learned this lesson at his own expense. Some claims cannot both be true, and the recognition of contradiction is the beginning of honest thinking. Name the conflict and you have already half-resolved it.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Han Feizi 韓非子·難一 (Nán Yī chapter)

Read the full reading
225

空kōng中zhōng樓lóu閣gé

kōng zhōng lóu gé

tower in the empty air

A castle in the air; an unrealistic dream or plan with no foundation.

The building suspended in cloud looks magnificent until you try to open the door. Beauty without foundation is a kind of elaborate deception, even when it is self-deception. The first question of any worthy aspiration is not how high but what is it standing on.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Song Dynasty 宋·沈括《夢溪筆談》 (Mèng Xī Bǐ Tán, Dream Pool Essays)

Read the full reading
226

三sān人rén行xíng必bì有yǒu我wǒ師shī

sān rén xíng bì yǒu wǒ shī

three walking there must be my teacher

Among any three people walking together, at least one has something to teach.

The one who carries arrogance into the room has already closed the classroom. In any grouping of people, someone is better at something than you are, has seen something you have not seen, or has made a mistake you have not yet made that could save you from making it. The humble eye finds teachers everywhere, because it is still looking for them.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·述而 (Shù Ér VII)

Read the full reading
227

不bù分fēn青qīng紅hóng皂zào白bái

bù fēn qīng hóng zào bái

not distinguish blue, red, dark, white

Without distinguishing right from wrong; indiscriminate; act without proper discernment.

The eye that sees no difference between the colors cannot help the painter and cannot serve the dyer. Discernment is not judgment in the harsh sense but the fundamental capacity to distinguish one thing from another clearly enough to act appropriately toward each. Without it, all responses are equally wrong.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 火 Fire 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common in folk and literary contexts)

Read the full reading
228

不bù怕pà不bù知zhī道dào，就jiù怕pà不bù學xué習xí

bù pà bù zhī dào jiù pà bù xué xí

not afraid of not knowing, just afraid of not studying

Ignorance is not the problem; the refusal to learn is.

Not knowing is the natural starting condition of the mind that has not yet encountered the thing it does not know. The refusal to learn is different: it is the choice to keep the ignorance, to actively maintain the gap. The first is normal; the second is a decision, and it has consequences that multiply with time. Not knowing is fine. Choosing to stay not knowing is not.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb

Read the full reading
229

不bù比bǐ不bù知zhī道dào，一yī比bǐ嚇xià一yī跳tiào

bù bǐ bù zhī dào yī bǐ xià yī tiào

don't compare don't know, one comparison get a fright

Without comparison you don't know the difference; comparing can reveal a shocking gap.

The thing that seemed adequate lived in the comfortable absence of comparison. The moment it is placed beside something genuinely excellent, the gap announces itself with some force. This shock is a gift: it locates exactly where the work is. The comparison that stings is the one that teaches.

俗語 Saying Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
230

出chū門mén看kàn天tiān色sè，進jìn門mén看kàn臉liǎn色sè

chū mén kàn tiān sè jìn mén kàn liǎn sè

leaving door look at sky color, entering door look at face color

Read the weather before going out; read people's moods before engaging with them.

The farmer who leaves the house without looking at the sky is not brave but unprepared. The person who enters a room without reading the emotional weather inside is the same. Both the sky and the face tell you what they are holding before they tell you anything else, and both are readable if you have learned to look. Read the room.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
231

前qián事shì不bù忘wàng，後hòu事shì之zhī師shī

qián shì bù wàng hòu shì zhī shī

not forgetting past matters, they become the teacher of future matters

Learn from the past; past experience, not forgotten, is a guide for the future.

The past that is kept in mind does not repeat itself as blindly as the past that is released from memory. Experience that is examined becomes instruction; experience that is merely lived through and then let go becomes the setup for the next repetition. Keep what happened close enough to be consulted. Memory is the teacher that requires the fewest credentials.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 水 Water 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Warring States 戰國策·趙策一 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Zhao Strategies I)

Read the full reading
232

學xué而ér不bù思sī則zé罔wǎng

xué ér bù sī zé wǎng

learning without thinking, one is lost

Study without reflection leaves you confused; the two must feed each other.

You have been collecting knowledge the way one hoards coins without spending, and something still feels empty about it. Facts that never pass through your own quiet questioning do not become yours, they just crowd the shelf. Sit with less, turn it over, and let it change you.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Analects 論語, Book 2

Read the full reading
233

山shān中zhōng無wú老lǎo虎hǔ，猴hóu子zi稱chēng大dà王wáng

shān zhōng wú lǎo hǔ hóu zi chēng dà wáng

when no tiger in the mountain, the monkey calls itself king

When capable people are absent, mediocre ones take charge; in the absence of the best, a lesser claims the throne.

The monkey's authority lasts exactly as long as the tiger's absence. The territory of leadership is always measured against the talent available to fill it, and what seems impressive with the mountain empty reveals its actual scale the moment a tiger returns. Know which kind of mountain you are standing on.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
234

授shòu人rén以yǐ魚yú不bù如rú授shòu人rén以yǐ漁yú

shòu rén yǐ yú bù rú shòu rén yǐ yú

give a person a fish not equal to teach a person fishing

Give a man a fish and you feed him for a day; teach him to fish and you feed him for a lifetime.

The meal handed over is a kindness; the skill transmitted is a different kind of gift entirely, one that reproduces itself. What we give to the hand is temporary but what we give to the hands that know how is a fire that keeps lighting itself. Teaching is the longer form of generosity.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Looks ahead 水 Water 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Attributed to Laozi tradition; common in Chinese educational philosophy

Read the full reading
235

格gé物wù致zhì知zhī

gé wù zhì zhī

investigate things to reach knowledge

Through careful study and investigation of the world, arrive at genuine understanding.

The naturalist who watches one species of beetle for a decade knows more about life than the philosopher who reads about it all afternoon. Knowledge rooted in the particular, in the actual held and examined thing, carries a weight that abstraction cannot match. Touch the world and let it teach you.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Da Xue 大學 (Dà Xué, The Great Learning)

Read the full reading
236

比bǐ中zhōng稱chēng良liáng

bǐ zhōng chēng liáng

by comparison the good is named

True quality is revealed by comparison; things are known by what they are measured against.

The jade is appreciated most after the stone has been held alongside it. What is genuinely good does not fear the comparison but is defined by it; the standard is part of what gives the excellent thing its name. Do not hide the excellent behind modesty that serves no one.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
237

溫wēn故gù而ér知zhī新xīn

wēn gù ér zhī xīn

warming the old to understand the new

Revisiting old knowledge with fresh eyes reveals new understanding; the past is not dead but alive with lessons.

The text you read at twenty will tell you something different when you read it at forty. Not because the text changed — because you did. The old knowledge holds the new insight in waiting, patient, until you have grown enough to receive it.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Analects 論語, Book 2 (Wei Zheng 為政, ch. 11)

Read the full reading
238

登dēng高gāo望wàng遠yuǎn

dēng gāo wàng yuǎn

ascend high, gaze far

Climb to a high place to see far; gain a broader perspective by rising above.

The summit does not come to the seeker; the seeker must make the ascent. And the view from the top is not simply a larger version of the view from below but a different kind of seeing entirely, one that corrects many things that seemed certain from the ground. Height is the beginning of wisdom about distance.

詩詞 Verse Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 火 Fire 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Wang Can 王粲·《登樓賦》 (Dēng Lóu Fù, Rhapsody on Climbing the Tower, Han Dynasty)

Read the full reading
239

聽tīng風fēng便biàn是shì雨yǔ

tīng fēng biàn shì yǔ

hear wind and take it as rain

Draw hasty conclusions from slight evidence; jump to conclusions.

Wind and rain are related but they are not the same thing, and the ear that cannot tell them apart will carry an umbrella through endless dry days. What sounds like confirmation often sounds like confirmation because we were already listening for it. Sit with the evidence a little longer before deciding what it is.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
240

隔gé行háng如rú隔gé山shān

gé háng rú gé shān

separate trade like separate mountain

Each profession is a world apart; expertise in one field gives little insight into another.

The distance between trades is not measured in feet but in years of particular attention, and the mountain between them is made of exactly that: the accumulated time of noticing the things that only that trade requires you to notice. The physician and the carpenter both know their mountain and neither knows the other's. This is not ignorance but specificity.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
241

一yī日rì為wéi師shī，終zhōng身shēn為wéi父fù

yī rì wéi shī zhōng shēn wéi fù

one day as teacher, lifelong as father

Even a single day of teaching creates a bond as deep as parenthood; honor your teachers.

The lesson received in a single afternoon can reorder an entire life, and the person who gave it deserves to be held with the same reverence as a parent, because what was transmitted was also a kind of birth. Those who shaped your thinking shaped your world as surely as those who shaped your body.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (common in educational culture)

Read the full reading
242

一yī箭jiàn雙shuāng雕diāo

yī jiàn shuāng diāo

one arrow two eagles

Kill two birds with one stone; achieve two goals with a single action.

The arrow let fly with perfect judgment catches what was aimed at and also what could not be aimed at but can be won by the same motion. True efficiency is not cutting corners but finding the angle where one act resonates outward into more than one good. The archer who reads the sky well needs fewer arrows.

成語 Adage Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Bei Shi 北史·長孫晟傳 (Cháng Sūn Shèng biography)

Read the full reading
243

忠zhōng言yán逆nì耳ěr利lì於yú行xíng

zhōng yán nì ěr lì yú xíng

loyal words grate on the ear but benefit the conduct

Honest, frank advice is uncomfortable to hear but invaluable for guiding right action.

Flattery is easy to receive and does nothing for you. The honest word that stings a little — that one changes your direction. The sting fades in a day. The correction it produces can last a lifetime. Seek the people who tell you the true thing.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Records of the Grand Historian 史記, Huaiyin Marquis (淮陰侯列傳); also Kongzi Jiayu 孔子家語

Read the full reading
244

良liáng禽qín擇zé木mù而ér棲qī

liáng qín zé mù ér qī

good bird chooses wood to perch on

A person of talent chooses where to work and who to serve; choose your environment wisely.

The excellent bird does not perch on every branch that appears. It circles and assesses before it chooses, because where it lands will shape how it lives and what it can become. Choosing the situation is as important as performing within it. The branch that seems lower and more convenient may be the wrong tree entirely.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 木 Wood 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Zuo Zhuan 左傳·哀公十一年 (Āi Gōng Year 11)

Read the full reading
245

小xiǎo心xīn無wú大dà錯cuò

xiǎo xīn wú dà cuò

careful heart, no big mistake

Care and caution prevent serious errors; the careful person makes few big mistakes.

The large error is almost always the child of a small carelessness that was not addressed when it was still small. Attention to the minor is not nitpicking but early intervention in the sequence that produces catastrophe. Those who stay careful in the small things are simply preventing the large things from having the raw material they need.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
246

人rén不bù可kě貌mào相xiāng，海hǎi水shuǐ不bù可kě斗dǒu量liáng

rén bù kě mào xiāng hǎi shuǐ bù kě dǒu liáng

person cannot be judged by appearance, seawater cannot be measured by a ladle

Don't judge by appearances; what seems small may be vast.

The ladle returned from the ocean is not wrong — it contains seawater — but it has understood nothing about the ocean itself. The first impression of a person is not false data but it is incomplete data, and conclusions drawn from it without further investigation will frequently be wrong in ways that matter. Take the second look.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Ming Dynasty 明·馮夢龍《醒世恆言》 (Xǐng Shì Héng Yán)

Read the full reading
247

魚yú與yǔ熊xióng掌zhǎng不bù可kě兼jiān得dé

yú yǔ xióng zhǎng bù kě jiān dé

fish and bear's paw cannot both be obtained

You cannot have everything; sometimes a choice must be made between two desirable things.

The table is only so wide, and the greatest meals ask for a decision. To refuse to choose is itself a choice, often the worst one. What we are willing to let go of defines the shape of what we get to keep.

諺語 Proverb Wisdom & Learning Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Mencius 孟子·告子上 (Gào Zǐ I)

Read the full reading
248

冰bīng清qīng玉yù潔jié

bīng qīng yù jié

ice clear, jade pure

Spotless and pure character; total moral integrity.

Ice does not negotiate with warmth; it either holds or it does not. The person whose values are as clear and cold and uncompromising as winter water occupies a rare position in the world: predictable in the best sense, which is to say trustworthy in the dark. What is truly pure does not require protection.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Qian Fu Lun 潛夫論·交際 (Jiāo Jì chapter, Wang Fu, Han Dynasty)

Read the full reading
249

張zhāng三sān李lǐ四sì

zhāng sān lǐ sì

Zhang Three, Li Four

Any random person; a common person from anywhere; Tom, Dick, or Harry.

There are ten thousand Zhang Threes and ten thousand Li Fours and all of them live ordinary lives with extraordinary interior dimensions that no catalog can capture. The ordinary person, the unnamed and uncelebrated one, is where most of the world's actual work gets done. Do not mistake anonymity for insignificance.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk expression (common usage since Song Dynasty)

Read the full reading
250

忙máng中zhōng不bù忘wàng善shàn

máng zhōng bù wàng shàn

in busyness do not forget goodness

Even in the middle of busy activity, do not forget to act with kindness.
