The busyness is often the excuse, but the one who has made virtue a habit does not need to remember to practice it in the rush because it has long since stopped being separate from the motion. Kindness that requires conditions to appear is fragile; kindness that persists through the chaos is the real kind.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
251

赤chì子zǐ之zhī心xīn

chì zǐ zhī xīn

the heart of a newborn infant

A pure, guileless heart without pretension; childlike sincerity and innocence.

The infant does not know yet how to perform itself for an audience. It cries when it is hungry and laughs when something delights it, without any calculation of the impression this makes. To keep this directness while becoming capable is the rare achievement of those we call truly good. The innocence that survives experience has been tested and earns its name.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Mencius 孟子·離婁下 (Lí Lóu II)

Read the full reading
252

人rén而ér無wú信xìn，不bù知zhī其qí可kě也yě

rén ér wú xìn, bù zhī qí kě yě

a person without trustworthiness — I do not know what he is good for

Without keeping one's word, a person cannot function among others at all — trust is the crossbar that lets the cart move.

Your word is the quietest thing you own and the most load-bearing. Break it in small ways and no one announces it, but the cart stops rolling anyway — people simply stop handing you the reins, and you rarely get told why.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Analects 論語, Wei Zheng 為政

Read the full reading
253

厚hòu德dé載zài物wù

hòu dé zài wù

thick virtue carries things

As the earth bears everything, deep virtue supports all around it.

The earth never refuses a thing set upon it. To carry others the way the ground carries you is not weakness; it is the deepest strength a person can hold.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source I Ching 易經, Kun hexagram commentary

Read the full reading
254

木mù板bǎn正zhèng行xíng，影yǐng必bì直zhí

mù bǎn zhèng xíng yǐng bì zhí

wooden board straight walk, shadow must be straight

When you act upright, your reputation follows suit; proper conduct produces proper results.

The shadow cannot be independently negotiated; it faithfully follows the object that casts it. What is done in the world leaves a trace in the world of opinion, and no amount of managing the trace can substitute for tending to what casts it. The best reputation management is living a life worth its reputation.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
255

沒méi有yǒu規guī矩jǔ，不bù成chéng方fāng圓yuán

méi yǒu guī jǔ bù chéng fāng yuán

without compass and square, cannot form circle and square

Without rules and standards, nothing can be properly built; structure enables achievement.

The circle requires the compass and the square requires the ruler, and without these instruments of form, what you have is not a circle or a square but an approximation of both that serves as neither. This is not a defense of rigidity but of precision: form makes the thing a thing rather than an intention. Rules are the grammar of accomplishment.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Mencius 孟子·離婁上 (Lí Lóu I)

Read the full reading
256

一yī視shì同tóng仁rén

yī shì tóng rén

one gaze, same benevolence

Treat everyone equally and with equal benevolence.

The sun does not measure who it warms each morning, and the rain falls on the field of the generous and the narrow alike. To see another person fully, as fully as you see yourself, is the practice that gradually dissolves the illusion that there is a them at all. This is the oldest form of justice.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Tang Dynasty 唐·韓愈《原人》 (Yuán Rén, Han Yu essay)

Read the full reading
257

得dé道dào多duō助zhù，失shī道dào寡guǎ助zhù

dé dào duō zhù shī dào guǎ zhù

gain the way, many help; lose the way, few help

Those who act rightly gain many allies; those who act wrongly stand alone.

Righteousness is not a performance but a field that draws others toward it like warmth draws people from the cold. The leader who acts in accord with what is true finds support appearing where none was visible before. Those who act against it discover that loyalty cannot be purchased, only earned.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Mencius 孟子·公孫丑下 (Gōng Sūn Chǒu II)

Read the full reading
258

疾jí風fēng知zhī勁jìng草cǎo

jí fēng zhī jìng cǎo

a fierce wind knows the sturdy grass

Only a hard wind reveals which grass is strong; only crisis shows whose loyalty and integrity truly hold.

When everything shook, you found out who bent flat and who stayed rooted — including yourself. The storm did not weaken you so much as tell you the truth about your own footing, and there is a strange relief in finally knowing.

俗語 Saying Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Book of the Later Han 後漢書, Biography of Wang Ba 王霸傳

Read the full reading
259

一yī年nián之zhī計jì在zài於yú春chūn，一yī日rì之zhī計jì在zài於yú晨chén

yī nián zhī jì zài yú chūn, yī rì zhī jì zài yú chén

a year's plan rests in spring; a day's plan rests in the morning

Begin at the beginning — the whole shape of a year or a day is set by what you do at its first hour.

Whatever the year becomes is decided in the thaw, and whatever the day becomes is decided before you are fully awake. The opening moment is doing quiet, permanent work while you think nothing has started yet, so plant early.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source folk proverb; recorded in 《增廣賢文》, echoing the Liang-dynasty 《纂要》

Read the full reading
260

人rén之zhī初chū，性xìng本běn善shàn

rén zhī chū xìng běn shàn

person at the beginning, nature originally good

Human nature at birth is fundamentally good; we are born innocent and good.

Before the world has had a chance to apply its lessons, what the child is is something uncontaminated. The original goodness is not naivety but the baseline from which everything else departs. What we recover toward, in our better moments, is something we already were.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks back 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source San Zi Jing 三字經 (Sān Zì Jīng, Three Character Classic)

Read the full reading
261

以yǐ和hé為wéi貴guì

yǐ hé wéi guì

treat harmony as most precious

Value harmony above all things; make peace the highest priority.

The most expensive thing in the room is often the agreement between the people in it. Harmony is not the absence of conflict but the presence of something that holds the people together across it, a willingness to find and maintain the shared ground. This is more precious than any object placed in the center of the space.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·學而 (Xué Ér I); also from 禮記

Read the full reading
262

勿wù以yǐ善shàn小xiǎo而ér不bù為wéi

wù yǐ shàn xiǎo ér bù wéi

do not think good is too small to do

Do not neglect small acts of goodness; every good deed matters no matter how small.

The one who waits to do good until they have something grand to offer rarely does good at all. The small act of goodness is the same goodness as the large one, just in a smaller container, and the practice of performing it in small amounts is the exercise that makes the capacity for larger ones possible. Do the small thing now.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source San Guo Zhi 三國志·先主傳 (Liú Bèi biography)

Read the full reading
263

玉yù潔jié冰bīng清qīng

yù jié bīng qīng

jade pure, ice clear

Pure and incorruptible character; spotless integrity like jade and ice.

What is cold and clear and holds its shape under pressure describes both ice and certain kinds of character. The purity is not absence but refusal — of compromise that would cloud, of warmth that would dissolve. The jade resting in a stream is cleaned by the water while remaining entirely itself.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Tang Dynasty 唐·王昌齡詩 (Wáng Chānglíng poetry)

Read the full reading
264

積jī善shàn成chéng丘qiū

jī shàn chéng qiū

accumulated goodness forms a hill

Small acts of goodness accumulate into something great; virtue builds through repeated small acts.

No one gesture of goodness builds the hill, but neither does the hill form without each one. The grain of sand knows nothing of the dune it is part of, and yet without it the dune is different. Every small act of decency is structural whether or not it receives credit. Goodness accumulates in the dark.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 土 Earth 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Xun Zi 荀子·勸學 (Quàn Xué, Encouraging Learning)

Read the full reading
265

笑xiào一yī笑xiào，十shí年nián少shào

xiào yī xiào, shí nián shào

one laugh, ten years younger

A light heart keeps the body young; laughter is medicine, and worry is what ages you.

The mirror ages faster for the anxious than for the amused — that is not a metaphor, it is your own face keeping score. Loosen something today; the laugh you almost swallowed was worth a decade you didn't know you were spending.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source folk health proverb (養生諺語); full form 笑一笑，十年少；愁一愁，白了頭

Read the full reading
266

與yǔ朋péng友yǒu交jiāo，言yán而ér有yǒu信xìn

yǔ péng yǒu jiāo, yán ér yǒu xìn

in dealings with friends, let your word be trustworthy

The core of friendship is that what you say can be relied on; a friend is someone whose promises you never have to double-check.

The friends who last are the ones whose "I'll be there" you never have to test. You are trying to be that for someone — the person whose small promises land like settled facts, so nobody has to hold their breath waiting to see if you meant it.

俗語 Saying Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Analects 論語, Xue Er 學而 (Zixia)

Read the full reading
267

錦jǐn上shàng添tiān花huā

jǐn shàng tiān huā

add flowers to brocade

Add beauty to what is already beautiful; make something good even better.

The brocade that was already magnificent receives the flower and becomes something different again, not replaced but enhanced. There is a generosity in knowing how to add to excellence without obscuring it, to contribute rather than compete with what is already there. This is the art of collaboration at its highest: leaving the original recognizably itself but better.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Song Dynasty 宋·王安石詩 (Wáng Ānshí poem usage)

Read the full reading
268

修xiū身shēn齊qí家jiā治zhì國guó平píng天tiān下xià

xiū shēn qí jiā zhì guó píng tiān xià

cultivate the self, regulate the family, govern the state, bring peace to the world

Personal cultivation is the root of all social order; start with yourself.

Everything begins in the same place: the room inside you that you have not yet tidied. The family, the village, the nation — all of them are outward expressions of what the person at the center has or has not done with themselves. The great work starts where you are standing.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Da Xue 大學 (Dà Xué, The Great Learning)

Read the full reading
269

公gōng道dào自zì在zài人rén心xīn

gōng dào zì zài rén xīn

justice is naturally in the human heart

People's hearts naturally know what is fair; justice lives in human consciousness.

The judgment of the crowd is not always just but the deep human instinct toward fairness is persistent, even when it is suppressed. What is genuinely right carries a resonance that ordinary people can sense without having studied the law, and those who act in accord with it tend to find the world's quiet majority on their side eventually. Justice is not invented; it is recognized.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
270

剛gāng柔róu相xiāng濟jì

gāng róu xiāng jì

hard and soft mutually aid

Strength and gentleness complement and balance each other.

Iron alone will shatter; silk alone will drift. The bow works because its wood knows when to yield and when to hold. Every lasting thing in this world is made of two forces in conversation, not one force winning.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Yi Jing 易經·繫辭 (Xì Cí, Commentary on Appended Judgments)

Read the full reading
271

天tiān意yì之zhī間jiān，在zài乎hū用yòng心xīn

tiān yì zhī jiān zài hū yòng xīn

heaven's intention in between, lies in using the heart

Between heaven's will and human fate lies the sincerity of the human heart.

The gap between what the sky ordains and what the person receives is not empty but filled with the quality of attention they brought. Heaven's intention is vast and impersonal; the heart applied to it makes it particular, personal, and answerable. Sincerity is the instrument through which the large passes into the life of the small.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese philosophical saying

Read the full reading
272

山shān高gāo水shuǐ長cháng

shān gāo shuǐ cháng

mountains high, waters long

Enduring like mountains and rivers; the lasting influence of a virtuous person.

The person of genuine virtue leaves behind something as stable as the mountain and as continuous as the river. Their influence does not diminish with their presence because it was not dependent on the theater of their being there; it was embedded in what they actually were. Long after, the mountain and the river remain, describing them.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Fan Zhongyan 范仲淹·《嚴先生祠堂記》 (Yán Xiānsheng Cí Táng Jì, Song Dynasty essay)

Read the full reading
273

萬wàn物wù並bìng育yù而ér不bù相xiāng害hài

wàn wù bìng yù ér bù xiāng hài

ten thousand things grow together and do not harm each other

All things can grow together without harming each other; the harmony of coexistence.

The forest does not decide which species gets to grow and which does not. The oak and the fern and the moss and the beetle find their place in the same system, each contributing something no other contributes, and the system is richer for the fullness. This is the ecology of genuine harmony: not agreement but coexistence that deepens rather than diminishes.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Li Ji 禮記·中庸 (Zhōng Yōng, Doctrine of the Mean)

Read the full reading
274

立lì德dé立lì功gōng立lì言yán

lì dé lì gōng lì yán

establish virtue, establish merit, establish words

The three immortalities: leave a legacy of virtue, great deeds, and wise writings.

Three things survive the person: what they were, what they did, and what they said that was worth repeating. The life that manages all three has nothing to mourn at its ending, having invested itself in forms that outlast the body. Not all three are equally available to every person, but at least one is available to everyone.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 金 Metal 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Zuozhuan 左傳·襄公二十四年 (Xiāng Gōng Year 24)

Read the full reading
275

一yī片piàn冰bīng心xīn在zài玉yù壺hú

yī piàn bīng xīn zài yù hú

one piece of ice heart in jade vessel

A pure and incorruptible heart kept clean within like ice in jade; integrity despite distance or difficulty.

The heart held pure like ice in the cold jade of the self remains unclouded by the heat of the world's opinions. Distance cannot dilute it, difficulty cannot discolor it, and the jade vessel does not require the world's approval to hold what it holds. This is what integrity looks like from the inside.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Wang Changling 王昌齡·《芙蓉樓送辛漸》 (Fú Róng Lóu Sòng Xīn Jiàn, Tang Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
276

勿wù以yǐ惡è小xiǎo而ér為wéi之zhī

wù yǐ è xiǎo ér wéi zhī

do not think the evil is small and do it

Do not commit an evil act just because it seems small; small wrongs grow.

The small wrong is not contained to the moment it occurs; it establishes a precedent, a permission, a slight lowering of the threshold for the next one. This is how the large wrong is built: not in one moment of obvious decision but in a series of small ones that each seemed negligible at the time. Attend to the small ones while they are still small.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source San Guo Zhi 三國志·先主傳 (Liú Bèi biography)

Read the full reading
277

士shì為wèi知zhī己jǐ者zhě死sǐ

shì wèi zhī jǐ zhě sǐ

a gentleman will die for the one who truly knows him

To be genuinely understood by another person is so rare that it can command a loyalty deeper than life — the debt owed to being truly seen.

There is one person who understood the thing about you that you never had to explain. That recognition landed somewhere below reason, and the loyalty it woke in you would frighten anyone watching from outside — but you know exactly what it is worth.

俗語 Saying Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks back 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Zhanguo Ce 戰國策; Records of the Grand Historian 史記, Yu Rang 豫讓

Read the full reading
278

路lù遙yáo知zhī馬mǎ力lì，日rì久jiǔ見jiàn人rén心xīn

lù yáo zhī mǎ lì, rì jiǔ jiàn rén xīn

a long road tests a horse's strength; long days reveal a person's heart

Character and loyalty are proven only over time — you cannot know someone's true heart until the road has been long enough to wear the pretense off.

Do not decide who someone is in the first season. Give it the long road — the boring stretches, the setbacks, the years — and watch what stays constant. Time is the one witness nobody can bribe.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Yuan-era drama / folk proverb (諺語); attested Wiktionary

Read the full reading
279

飯fàn後hòu百bǎi步bù走zǒu，活huó到dào九jiǔ十shí九jiǔ

fàn hòu bǎi bù zǒu, huó dào jiǔ shí jiǔ

walk a hundred steps after a meal, live to ninety-nine

Small, gentle, regular movement is the quiet secret of a long life; don't sit still after you eat.

Longevity is rarely dramatic. It hides in the hundred unhurried steps after supper, the ordinary habit you could skip a thousand times without noticing — until, at the far end, you notice you are still here.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source folk health proverb (養生諺語)

Read the full reading
280

君jūn子zǐ之zhī交jiāo淡dàn如rú水shuǐ

jūn zǐ zhī jiāo dàn rú shuǐ

the friendship of the noble person is bland as water

True friendship between people of character is plain and unforced — not sweetened by flattery or gain, and lasting precisely because it asks for nothing.

You have a friend you can go months without calling, and pick up mid-sentence when you do. There is nothing to perform between you, nothing owed, and that flatness other people mistake for distance is the actual shape of trust — clear, drinkable, and it never runs out.

俗語 Saying Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Zhuangzi 莊子, chapter Shan Mu 山木

Read the full reading
281

和hé而ér不bù同tóng

hé ér bù tóng

harmonious yet not identical

Harmony does not require uniformity; one can be in accord without being the same.

The orchestra is not one instrument played together but many instruments playing differently toward the same silence between notes. Agreement does not require sameness, only a shared willingness to listen while making one's own sound. This is the difference between harmony and echo.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·子路 (Zǐ Lù XIII)

Read the full reading
282

己jǐ所suǒ不bù欲yù，勿wù施shī於yú人rén

jǐ suǒ bù yù, wù shī yú rén

what you do not want for yourself, do not impose on others

The Confucian rule of reciprocity; treat others as you would wish to be treated.

The sharp thing you are about to hand someone is a thing you have flinched from receiving yourself. You know exactly how it lands, because it once landed on you. Before you pass it on, ask whether you would open your own hand for it.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Analects 論語

Read the full reading
283

春chūn風fēng化huà雨yǔ

chūn fēng huà yǔ

spring wind transforms into rain

Subtle, gentle influence that nourishes and transforms; kind and effective education.

The spring wind does not announce its arrival or demand gratitude for the warmth it carries. It simply moves through the orchard and the thing that was tight and closed yesterday opens today. Influence that arrives as gentleness reaches farther than influence that arrives as force.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Mencius 孟子·盡心上 (Jìn Xīn I)

Read the full reading
284

水shuǐ滿mǎn則zé溢yì

shuǐ mǎn zé yì

water full then overflows

When something reaches its peak, it must decline; excess leads to loss.

The cup does not argue with the law of fullness; when it is full, it spills. Those who understand this pour out a little before the overflow and call it wisdom rather than waste. The art is in knowing exactly where full ends and too much begins, and not waiting for the flood to tell you.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Shi Ji 史記·范雎蔡澤列傳 (Fàn Suī Cài Zé biography)

Read the full reading
285

禮lǐ之zhī用yòng，和hé為wéi貴guì

lǐ zhī yòng hé wéi guì

in the application of ritual, harmony is most precious

The highest purpose of ritual and social norms is to create harmony.

The ceremony is not the point; the harmony the ceremony creates is the point. Form in service of warmth, structure in service of ease — this is the paradox that good social design achieves. The ritual that produces discord has misunderstood its own purpose as thoroughly as a medicine that makes the patient worse.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·學而 (Xué Ér I)

Read the full reading
286

以yǐ身shēn作zuò則zé

yǐ shēn zuò zé

use body as example

Lead by personal example.

The teacher who merely speaks of virtue and the one who lives it occupy different rooms entirely. A body in motion through the world leaves a trace that words cannot. Children learn what they watch, and so does everyone else.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·子路 (Zǐ Lù XIII)

Read the full reading
287

海hǎi內nèi存cún知zhī己jǐ，天tiān涯yá若ruò比bǐ鄰lín

hǎi nèi cún zhī jǐ, tiān yá ruò bǐ lín

if within the seas you have one who knows you, the ends of the earth are like next door

A true friend collapses distance; when someone knows your heart, being far apart feels like living in the same neighborhood.

The person who knows you best may live in another time zone, and it changes nothing. Distance is only cruel to acquaintances — for the one who understands you, a thousand miles reads like the width of a shared wall.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Wang Bo 王勃, 送杜少府之任蜀州 (Tang poem)

Read the full reading
288

清qīng松sōng之zhī節jié

qīng sōng zhī jié

pure pine's integrity

The integrity of the pine; moral uprightness that does not bend to circumstance.

The pine does not decide to stay green when the temperature drops; it simply is what it is under all conditions. Character that holds in warmth but changes in cold was never quite character at all. The pine is not admired for its effort to remain itself but for never considering the alternative.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Traditional Chinese folk metaphor (pine as symbol of integrity)

Read the full reading
289

過guò猶yóu不bù及jí

guò yóu bù jí

going too far is same as not reaching

Going too far is as bad as not going far enough; excess is as problematic as deficiency.

The salt that perfects the dish and the salt that ruins it are the same salt; only the amount differs. What distinguishes the master is not abundance of any quality but the precision of its application. Enough is not the same as more, and the space between them is exactly where the art lives.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·先進 (Xiān Jìn XI)

Read the full reading
290

雁yàn過guò留liú聲shēng，人rén過guò留liú名míng

yàn guò liú shēng rén guò liú míng

geese passing leave a cry, people passing leave a name

Geese leave behind their call; people leave behind their reputation. How you live is what remains.

The wild geese do not know they are leaving sound behind them; it is simply what their passing does. People, similarly, leave a residue of how they moved through the world, and those who were there after the departure can read it. What remains is not the monument you intended but the trace you actually made. Live accordingly.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
291

以yǐ直zhí報bào怨yuàn，以yǐ德dé報bào德dé

yǐ zhí bào yuàn yǐ dé bào dé

repay resentment with straightforwardness, repay virtue with virtue

Meet grievances with honest fairness, not with hatred or excess; return kindness for kindness.

Confucius was asked whether one should repay injury with kindness, and he said no: repay injury with straightforwardness, and kindness with kindness. The distinction matters. To respond to wrong with undifferentiated goodness is to erase the difference between wrong and right. Honest uprightness — seeing clearly what happened and responding without distortion in either direction — is what justice looks like in daily life.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·憲問 (Xiàn Wèn XIV)

Read the full reading
292

先xiān義yì後hòu利lì

xiān yì hòu lì

first righteousness, then benefit

Put righteousness before profit; moral principles come before material gain.

The person who asks what is right before asking what is profitable lives in a larger world than the one who reverses the order. Profit that arrives through righteousness sits comfortably with its owner; profit that arrives through other means arrives with a passenger. Put the question of right first, and the question of benefit answers itself more cleanly.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese Confucian principle (common in moral philosophy)

Read the full reading
293

善shàn惡è到dào頭tóu終zhōng有yǒu報bào

shàn è dào tóu zhōng yǒu bào

good and evil at the end always have recompense

Good and evil will both eventually receive their due; karma comes to all.

The accounting does not close at the moment we think it should, which is why patience is required alongside the conviction that it closes at all. Good received what it has coming, and evil the same, though the timeline is rarely the one we would have chosen. Trust the arc; it is longer than our frustration with it.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 火 Fire 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (common in theatrical and folk culture)

Read the full reading
294

報bào怨yuàn以yǐ德dé

bào yuàn yǐ dé

repay grievance with virtue

Return kindness for resentment; respond to injury with virtue.

The flame that is met with more flame grows until it burns everything down. The grievance met with virtue receives something it was not prepared for and often does not know how to continue being a grievance in the presence of it. This is not weakness but a different kind of strength: the one that changes the equation entirely.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第七十九章 (Chapter 79); also Analects 論語·憲問

Read the full reading
295

心xīn安ān理lǐ得dé

xīn ān lǐ dé

heart at peace, reason obtained

Have a clear conscience; feel morally justified and at ease with one's actions.

The person who has acted according to their values and can account for every step of their reasoning sleeps without argument. Conscience at rest is not comfort with mediocrity but the particular peace of having done what was right, clearly, and being able to say so in full light. This kind of peace cannot be counterfeited.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in moral and social contexts)

Read the full reading
296

心xīn正zhèng不bù怕pà影yǐng子zi斜xié

xīn zhèng bù pà yǐng zi xié

heart upright not fear shadow crooked

A clear conscience fears no accusation; the honest need not fear appearances.

The shadow is always slightly distorted by the angle of the light, but the body that casts it knows what it is and does not confuse itself with its shadow. A clean conscience is not innocence from all imperfection but alignment between what one is and what one does. Those who live in that alignment step into any light without flinching.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
297

患huàn難nàn見jiàn真zhēn情qíng

huàn nàn jiàn zhēn qíng

in hardship one sees true feeling

Adversity reveals who your real friends are; genuine bonds show themselves only when there is a cost to staying.

Count the ones who called when the news was bad, not when it was good. That short, unflattering list is the truth of your life, and you are on someone else's short list too — probably without knowing it.

俗語 Saying Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Chinese folk proverb (俗語); attested Wiktionary

Read the full reading
298

歲suì寒hán，然rán後hòu知zhī松sōng柏bǎi之zhī後hòu凋diāo也yě

suì hán, rán hòu zhī sōng bǎi zhī hòu diāo yě

only when the year turns cold do we know the pine and cypress are the last to wither

Character shows in hard seasons; the ones who hold their color when everything else fades are revealed only by the cold.

All summer the pine looks no greener than the leaves that will abandon the branch by November. Wait for the frost, and you will see who was evergreen the whole time — and cold is simply the season that stops the pretending.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 狗 Year of the Dog

Source 《論語·子罕》 (Analects 9.28)

Read the full reading
299

為wéi人rén不bù作zuò虧kuī心xīn事shì

wéi rén bù zuò kuī xīn shì

being a person, do not do things that trouble the conscience

Act in such a way that your conscience is clear; do not act against your own moral sense.

The thing that troubles the conscience at three in the morning is not confused about its nature, even if the daylight arguments were convincing. Act in such a way that the midnight review finds nothing that needs defending against your own witness. The only court that cannot be appealed is the inner one.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 金 Metal 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
300

井jǐng水shuǐ不bù犯fàn河hé水shuǐ

jǐng shuǐ bù fàn hé shuǐ

well water does not disturb river water

Mind one's own business; each thing stays in its own domain without encroaching on another's.

The well and the river are both water and they have never met, each sufficient in its own basin. This is not coldness but wisdom: there are domains that do not require overlap, and the refusal to trespass is itself a form of respect. Clear boundaries between streams keeps both streams clean.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
301

以yǐ德dé報bào怨yuàn

yǐ dé bào yuàn

with virtue repay resentment

Repay injury with kindness; respond to hostility with virtue and goodwill.

The response that is better than the provocation changes the nature of the exchange. To meet anger with virtue is not passivity but transformation: the situation that expected to continue as it was has been handed something it cannot continue with. The escalation can only happen if both sides agree to it.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Tao Te Ching 道德經·第六十三章 (Chapter 63)

Read the full reading
302

入rù鄉xiāng隨suí俗sú

rù xiāng suí sú

enter the village, follow its customs

When you are a guest somewhere, adapt to the local ways; respect the practices of wherever you find yourself.

The traveler who arrives demanding that a new place be like the old one has not truly arrived anywhere. To enter a place is to agree to be changed by it slightly. That willingness is not weakness — it is the whole point of going somewhere new.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Chinese folk proverb 民間諺語; medieval 佛祖統紀 (Buddhist chronicle)

Read the full reading
303

助zhù人rén為wéi樂lè

zhù rén wéi lè

helping people as joy

Find happiness in helping others.

The gift given without expectation of return carries a strange warmth that lingers longer than the giving. Those who seek their own joy by feeding another's need discover a source that does not empty. The well of generosity is one of the few that refills itself from the act of drawing.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
304

博bó愛ài之zhī謂wèi仁rén

bó ài zhī wèi rén

broad love is called benevolence

Broad love for all people is the definition of benevolence.

Benevolence is not a feeling reserved for the deserving or the familiar. It is a disposition toward all of life, the way the sun makes no distinctions about what it warms. What distinguishes the person of genuine benevolence is not the warmth they feel for those they like but the warmth they maintain for everyone, including those who have given them no particular reason.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Han Yu 韓愈·《原道》 (Yuán Dào, On the Origin of the Way, Tang Dynasty)

Read the full reading
305

善shàn花huā結jié善shàn果guǒ

shàn huā jié shàn guǒ

good flowers yield good fruit

Good intentions and actions produce good results.

The blossom declares the tree's intention, and the fruit confirms it. What is planted in goodness tends to flower in goodness and fruit in goodness, because this is the logic of organic systems: output reflects input across the long arc. Good flowers do not always guarantee good weather, but they are the right beginning for good fruit.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Looks ahead 木 Wood 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
306

得dé饒ráo人rén處chù且qiě饒ráo人rén

dé ráo rén chù qiě ráo rén

where you can spare a person, spare the person

Be lenient when possible; show mercy when you have the power to do so.

The power to destroy something is not an argument for destroying it. The road you leave open for the person you spare is also the road that eventually allows them to return what you gave them, or at least allows the world to note what you chose when you had the choice. Mercy extended is always a form of surplus, and surplus is never a waste.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
307

心xīn誠chéng則zé靈líng

xīn chéng zé líng

heart sincere then efficacious

When the heart is sincere, prayers and efforts will be answered.

The prayer spoken with a divided heart moves nothing. The same words offered from a place of complete sincerity arrive differently and seem to land somewhere. Whether the listening is from within or without, genuine intention creates a different kind of signal than performance does. Sincerity is not a feeling but a direction of one's whole attention.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (common in religious and folk contexts)

Read the full reading
308

成chéng人rén之zhī美měi，不bù成chéng人rén之zhī惡è

chéng rén zhī měi bù chéng rén zhī è

help complete others' goodness, do not help complete others' evil

Support others in what is good; do not assist others in what is harmful.

The same hands that can plant can pull up, and it matters profoundly which capacity they exercise toward which thing. To help the good emerge and to refuse to help the harmful grow is not neutrality but choice, the most basic form of moral participation in the world around us. Assistance is always a vote for something.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·顏淵 (Yán Yuān XII)

Read the full reading
309

桃táo李lǐ不bù言yán，下xià自zì成chéng蹊xī

táo lǐ bù yán xià zì chéng xī

peach and plum do not speak, below naturally forms a path

True virtue needs no advertisement; people are naturally drawn to what is genuinely good.

The peach tree does not announce that it has peaches. It does not need to. The sweetness does the work; the path wears itself into the earth from the repeated feet of those who came and came again. Authentic value creates its own attention, and what must be loudly advertised rarely has the depth to keep what the noise attracts.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 木 Wood 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Shi Ji 史記·李將軍列傳 (Lǐ Jiāngjūn biography)

Read the full reading
310

為wéi善shàn最zuì樂lè

wéi shàn zuì lè

doing good is greatest joy

The greatest happiness comes from doing good; virtue brings true joy.

The delight that comes from having done something genuinely good is qualitatively different from other pleasures. It does not fade by morning, it does not require repetition to feel valid, and it does not depend on anyone watching. This is the joy that keeps, and it is available in any moment to anyone.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Dong Han Shu 東漢書·東平王蒼傳 (Dōng Píng Wáng Cāng biography)

Read the full reading
311

與yǔ人rén為wéi善shàn

yǔ rén wéi shàn

with others, act with goodness

Be kind to people; approach others with a generous and benevolent spirit.

Goodness given away does not diminish the giver. It does something stranger — it accumulates. The person known for treating others well finds that others want to treat them well in return, until the whole arrangement becomes self-sustaining.

成語 Adage Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Mencius 孟子, Gongsun Chou I 公孫丑上

Read the full reading
312

衆zhòng人rén拾shí柴chái火huǒ焰yàn高gāo

zhòng rén shí chái huǒ yàn gāo

when many people gather firewood, the flames leap high

When everyone contributes even a small effort, the collective result is far greater than what any one person could achieve alone.

No single piece of firewood makes a fire. But the same piece, placed among the others, becomes part of something that can warm a whole house. The contribution looks small until you see what it is part of.

諺語 Proverb Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 火 Fire 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Chinese folk proverb 民間諺語

Read the full reading
313

飲yǐn食shí有yǒu節jié，起qǐ居jū有yǒu常cháng

yǐn shí yǒu jié, qǐ jū yǒu cháng

eat and drink with measure, rise and rest with regularity

Health is built on moderation and rhythm — temperate at the table, steady in the hours you keep.

The body keeps no secrets; it silently tallies every late night and every meal you didn't need. Feed it within its measure and let it wake and sleep by a rhythm it can trust, and it will spend those saved years back on you.

詩詞 Verse Harmony, Virtue & Balance Present-minded 土 Earth 豬 Year of the Pig

Source 《黃帝內經·素問·上古天真論》 (Huangdi Neijing / Yellow Emperor's Inner Canon)

Read the full reading
314

一yī寸cùn光guāng陰yīn一yī寸cùn金jīn

yī cùn guāng yīn yī cùn jīn

one inch of time, one inch of gold

Time is as precious as gold; an inch of time is worth an inch of gold.

The coin can be replaced; the hour cannot. Gold is dug from the earth each year in new quantities, but the hour that passed while you were elsewhere is the one hour in all of history that was exactly that hour, and it is now only the past tense of itself. Treat each portion of time as the finite and non-renewable thing it is.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 金 Metal 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Wang Zhong 汪中 (Tang period attribution); very common Chinese maxim

Read the full reading
315

一yī本běn萬wàn利lì

yī běn wàn lì

one capital, ten-thousand profit

A tiny investment yields an enormous return.

You keep waiting for the grand stake, the sum that changes everything at once. But the fortunes that hold were rarely the largest ones laid down — they were the smallest ones placed exactly right, then left alone to swell in the dark.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 金 Metal 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Qing dynasty novel 市聲 (Shì Shēng) by 姬文, ch. 26; common merchant idiom

Read the full reading
316

今jīn天tiān事shì，今jīn天tiān畢bì

jīn tiān shì jīn tiān bì

today's matters, today completed

Finish today's work today; don't push today's tasks to tomorrow.

The work not completed today carries a weight into the next day that compounds. The morning that begins clear of yesterday's incompleteness has a different quality of readiness, a different relationship to what will be asked of it. Today's task completed today is the gift given to tomorrow before tomorrow has even asked for it.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 木 Wood 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (common in educational and work culture)

Read the full reading
317

鐵tiě杵chǔ磨mó針zhēn

tiě chǔ mó zhēn

grind an iron pestle into a needle

Sustained diligence achieves what looks impossible.

You looked at the size of what you are trying to become and called it impossible, and from where you stand today you are right. But impossibility is a statement about a single afternoon, not about ten thousand of them. Pick up the stone.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 金 Metal 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source 方輿勝覽 (Li Bai legend)

Read the full reading
318

一yī分fēn耕gēng耘yún，一yī分fēn收shōu穫huò

yī fēn gēng yún, yī fēn shōu huò

one measure of tilling, one measure of harvest

The reward you reap is exactly proportional to the effort you sow.

The field keeps honest accounts. It will not pay you for the mornings you meant to work, only for the ones you knelt in the dirt. What you gather in autumn is simply spring, returned to you with interest and no lies.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Common Chinese proverb; recorded in the Taiwan MOE 教育部 idiom dictionary

Read the full reading
319

一yī分fēn耕gēng耘yún一yī分fēn收shōu穫huò

yī fēn gēng yún yī fēn shōu huò

one part plowing and weeding, one part harvest

The harvest is proportional to the effort; what you put in determines what you get out.

The field keeps honest accounts. It does not reward good intentions that remained in the house, nor punish bad luck that happened elsewhere. It returns what was given to it in the form of work: the tilled depth, the weeding done, the irrigation maintained. Not more, not less. This honesty is what makes the field a trustworthy teacher about how the world actually works beneath the noise of fortune.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese agricultural proverb

Read the full reading
320

一yī生shēng勤qín儉jiǎn，萬wàn事shì可kě成chéng

yī shēng qín jiǎn wàn shì kě chéng

a lifetime of diligence and frugality, ten thousand matters can be accomplished

A life of hard work and thrift makes all things possible.

The life organized around what is genuinely needed, without excess, and the work applied consistently without interruption, compounds quietly over the years into a foundation that surprises even the one who built it. This is not the exciting story but it is the complete one: the whole life spent well is the whole life achieved. Nothing withheld, everything given, everything possible.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk wisdom (common in family tradition)

Read the full reading
321

勤qín勞láo致zhì富fù

qín láo zhì fù

diligence and labor bring wealth

Hard work leads to prosperity; wealth comes from persistent effort.

The field of the hardworking farmer does not look different from any other field in March, but by September it tells a different story. The gap between the harvest of the diligent and the harvest of the careless is not the land but the labor, and the labor is not talent but choice, made again and again on the mornings when it would have been easier to stay inside.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
322

勤qín能néng補bǔ拙zhuō

qín néng bǔ zhuō

diligence can mend clumsiness

Hard work compensates for a lack of natural talent.

You were not the quickest in the room; you have known that a long time. But quickness spends itself early, and the one who keeps returning to the work, clumsy and unhurried, ends up holding what the gifted let slip. Effort is the talent no one is born without.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Associated with Bai Juyi 白居易 (Tang); popularized via 邵雍 and later usage — classical idiom, exact locus debated

Read the full reading
323

種zhòng瓜guā得dé瓜guā，種zhòng豆dòu得dé豆dòu

zhòng guā dé guā zhòng dòu dé dòu

plant melon get melon, plant bean get bean

You reap what you sow; actions have natural and predictable consequences.

The field does not negotiate. What goes in comes out, and the season does not apologize for returning exactly what was committed to the ground. There is a deep fairness in this — not the fairness of things working out, but the fairness of honest accounting. Act accordingly.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 木 Wood 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
324

積jī少shǎo成chéng多duō

jī shǎo chéng duō

accumulate few, become many

Many small things gathered steadily become much; abundance is built grain by grain.

No one gathers a harvest in a day. Abundance is patient arithmetic, a little added and a little kept, until the small becomes a weight you can lean on.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Book of Han 漢書

Read the full reading
325

腳jiǎo踏tà實shí地dì

jiǎo tà shí dì

feet tread solid ground

Keep both feet on the ground; be practical and down-to-earth.

The highest aspiration still requires a foot that knows where it is standing. Dreams are navigated from the ground, not from the air, and the person whose soles keep honest contact with the earth rarely loses their way in the clouds. What is most real begins exactly where you are.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Song Dynasty 宋·邵雍《二程外書》 (Èr Chéng Wài Shū)

Read the full reading
326

鍥qiè而ér不bù捨shě

qiè ér bù shě

to carve and not let go

To keep chiseling without ever giving up, until even stone yields.

Set the blade down and rotten wood defeats you. Keep carving and metal and stone give way. The difference was never the hardness of the material — it was whether your hand came back to it the next day, and the next.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 金 Metal 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Xunzi 荀子, 勸學 (Exhortation to Learning): 鍥而不舍，金石可鏤; Warring States period

Read the full reading
327

開kāi源yuán節jié流liú

kāi yuán jié liú

open the source, restrain the flow

Widen your streams of income while curbing what drains away.

Two hands, always. One opens the spring wider; the other quietly narrows the leak you have been pretending not to see. You cannot fill a vessel you refuse to mend.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 水 Water 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Xunzi 荀子, 富國 (Enriching the State), Warring States period

Read the full reading
328

事shì在zài人rén為wéi

shì zài rén wéi

the matter lies in what a person does

Whether a thing succeeds depends on human effort, not on fate.

You keep asking whether it is meant to be, as if the answer were sealed somewhere out of reach. It is not sealed. The thing waits to see what you will do about it. Fate hands you the clay; the shape is your affair.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 火 Fire 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Feng Menglong 馮夢龍, 東周列國志 (Chronicles of the Eastern Zhou States), ch. 69, Ming dynasty

Read the full reading
329

事shì半bàn功gōng倍bèi

shì bàn gōng bèi

effort half, achievement double

Achieve twice the result with half the effort; work smarter by finding the path of least resistance.

The room that drains you asks for more and more and still stays dim. The room that feeds you asks for almost nothing and somehow holds light all day. The difference is not the hours poured in but the angle the work enters from.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Mencius 孟子, Gongsun Chou I 公孫丑上

Read the full reading
330

守shǒu株zhū待dài兔tù

shǒu zhū dài tù

guard the stump, wait for the rabbit

A caution against idle hope where effort is needed; arrange your space to act, not to wait.

Luck that came once will not return to the same still hand. Arrange your life to move toward what you want, not to sit by the stump waiting for it to fall again.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Han Feizi 韓非子, Five Vermin

Read the full reading
331

八bā方fāng來lái風fēng，利lì在zài當dāng中zhōng

bā fāng lái fēng lì zài dāng zhōng

wind coming from eight directions, benefit is at the center

When resources and support flow from all directions, the greatest advantage lies at the center where they converge.

The hub does not go to where the spokes are; the spokes come to it. The person or place that becomes a genuine center of convergence does not need to reach in eight directions because the eight directions already lean inward. What draws flows in; what positions itself correctly receives from everywhere. Being the center is the quietest form of abundance.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common in business and prosperity culture)

Read the full reading
332

貨huò悖bèi而ér入rù者zhě亦yì悖bèi而ér出chū

huò bèi ér rù zhě yì bèi ér chū

goods that come in crookedly also go out crookedly

Wealth gained by wrong means departs by wrong means.

Money remembers how it was earned. What arrives through a crooked door has already learned the way out, and it will leave the same night you stop watching it, taking a little of your peace as fare.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source The Great Learning 大學 (in the Book of Rites 禮記), ch. 10

Read the full reading
333

人rén生shēng在zài勤qín，不bù索suǒ何hé獲huò

rén shēng zài qín bù suǒ hé huò

life is in diligence, not seek what gain

Life's meaning lies in diligence; without effort, what can one gain?

The field does not reward the person who watches it. What is gained without the seeking is accidental, and what is sought without diligence is usually not found. The life shaped by steady work has a particular solidity that the windfall life does not, a sense of deserving what has arrived.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Zhang Heng 張衡·《應閒》 (Yìng Xián, Han Dynasty essay)

Read the full reading
334

君jūn子zǐ愛ài財cái，取qǔ之zhī有yǒu道dào

jūn zǐ ài cái qǔ zhī yǒu dào

the noble person loves wealth, but obtains it by proper means

Even a person of virtue may desire wealth, but must acquire it honestly; the means must match the goal.

The desire for prosperity is not shameful; what carries it is. The person of principle does not close their eyes to resources but opens them only along the paths that their own conscience can accompany. The wealth acquired by means the owner would not name in public is already spending itself in the form of the secret it keeps.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 金 Metal 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (very common in business moral culture)

Read the full reading
335

天tiān道dào酬chóu勤qín

tiān dào chóu qín

the way of heaven rewards diligence

Heaven repays those who labor without slackening.

You will not always see who is keeping score, and some nights it feels like no one is. But the sky has a long memory for effort. Keep working when the reward is nowhere in sight; the ledger is being kept in an ink you cannot read yet.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Song–Ming Neo-Confucian maxim built on the Book of Changes 周易 (乾卦, 天行健); phrase itself is later formulation

Read the full reading
336

沒méi有yǒu不bù勞láo而ér獲huò

méi yǒu bù láo ér huò

there is no getting without working

Nothing comes without effort; there is no free lunch.

The harvest that arrives without the planting is called theft. Everything has a cost, and the cost that was not paid in effort was paid in something else: integrity, opportunity, or the development that the work itself would have produced. The shortcut deposits you somewhere, but not at the destination of the full effort.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb

Read the full reading
337

敬jìng業yè樂lè群qún

jìng yè lè qún

respect work, find joy in the community

Take your work seriously and find delight in your community; professional dedication combined with social joy.

The person who takes their work seriously and loves the people around the work has found the complete version of a professional life. Neither alone is sufficient: grave work without social warmth is lonely, and sociability without seriousness about the work is shallow. The combination produces the particular fullness of someone who is both good at what they do and glad to be doing it among others.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 火 Fire 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Li Ji 禮記·學記 (Xué Jì, Record of Learning)

Read the full reading
338

明míng日rì復fù明míng日rì，明míng日rì何hé其qí多duō

míng rì fù míng rì míng rì hé qí duō

tomorrow again tomorrow, how many tomorrows there are

Beware of endless procrastination; there will always be another tomorrow, until there isn't.

Tomorrow has never yet arrived to do what was left for it. It always arrives as today, wearing today's face and full of today's other commitments, and the thing that was going to happen tomorrow becomes the thing going to happen tomorrow again. The only day that exists for doing is the day that is currently happening. Today is the only tomorrow that ever arrived.

詩詞 Verse Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Wen Jia 文嘉·《明日歌》 (Míng Rì Gē, Song of Tomorrow, Ming Dynasty)

Read the full reading
339

業yè精jīng於yú勤qín荒huāng於yú嬉xī

yè jīng yú qín huāng yú xī

mastery is perfected by diligence, laid waste by play

Skill is sharpened through hard work and ruined through idle amusement.

Your craft is a garden with no neutral season. On the days you tend it, it deepens; on the days you drift, it does not merely pause — it goes to weed. There is no standing still in a thing you love; you are always either sharpening it or letting it dull.

詩詞 Verse Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Han Yu 韓愈, 進學解 (Exhortation on Study), Tang dynasty

Read the full reading
340

一yī日rì之zhī計jì在zài於yú晨chén

yī rì zhī jì zài yú chén

a day's plan lies in the morning

Plan the day in the morning; the early hours are the most important for setting the day's direction.

The morning is not merely early; it is when the day has not yet become anything in particular, when it is still available, still open to being shaped. The hour before the world's claims arrive is the one where intention can be set before the current takes over. What is planned in stillness holds through the noise.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 木 Wood 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Northern Qi Dynasty 北齊·顏之推《顏氏家訓》 (Yán Shì Jiā Xùn, Yanshi Family Instructions)

Read the full reading
341

工gōng欲yù善shàn其qí事shì，必bì先xiān利lì其qí器qì

gōng yù shàn qí shì bì xiān lì qí qì

a craftsman who wishes to do good work must first sharpen his tools

To do any task well, you must first ensure your tools, preparation, and conditions are properly in place.

The hour spent sharpening is not time taken from the work — it is time given to the work before the work begins. The craftsperson who knows this is never in a hurry at the start, and rarely in trouble in the middle.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Analects 論語, Book 15 (Wei Ling Gong 衛靈公)

Read the full reading
342

早zǎo起qǐ的de鳥niǎo兒ér有yǒu蟲chóng吃chī

zǎo qǐ de niǎo ér yǒu chóng chī

the early rising bird has worms to eat

The early bird catches the worm; those who start first gain advantage.

The worm is not saved until a bird worth catching it comes along, and the bird that is still in the nest when the field is active will find the field already picked over. Being early is not being desperate but being aligned with the natural rhythm of when things become available, which is usually earlier than the comfortable schedule allows.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 木 Wood 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
343

書shū中zhōng自zì有yǒu黃huáng金jīn屋wū

shū zhōng zì yǒu huáng jīn wū

within books there is, of itself, a house of gold

Learning is its own treasury — study opens the door to fortune.

You will not find the gold by shaking the book. It is in the slow reading, the nights the page would not release you. What you learn becomes a room no one can repossess, and later, when you least expect it, you walk in and find it furnished.

詩詞 Verse Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Emperor Zhenzong of Song 宋真宗, 勵學篇 / 勸學詩 (Encouragement to Study), Song dynasty

Read the full reading
344

磨mó刀dāo不bù誤wù砍kǎn柴chái工gōng

mó dāo bù wù kǎn chái gōng

sharpening the blade does not delay the woodcutting work

Taking time to prepare properly does not waste time; it saves more time than it spends.

The dull blade takes three times as long and leaves you tired and still not done. The sharp blade finishes before you expect it and leaves energy for what comes next. Preparation is not delay — it is the first move of the work itself.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 金 Metal 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Chinese folk proverb 民間諺語; Mao Zedong used this phrase in 1943

Read the full reading
345

恪kè盡jìn職zhí守shǒu

kè jìn zhí shǒu

respectfully fulfill duty and keep

Faithfully and carefully fulfill one's duties; be conscientious in one's work.

The one who tends the post through the night hours when no supervisor watches has understood something about duty that those who perform it only in daylight have not. Work that is completed the same way whether or not anyone is looking is work that has been taken seriously as its own reason. This is professionalism in its deepest sense.

成語 Adage Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 狗 Year of the Dog

Source Traditional Chinese idiom (common in official and professional contexts)

Read the full reading
346

和hé氣qì生shēng財cái

hé qì shēng cái

harmonious spirit generates wealth

A harmonious atmosphere and good relationships bring prosperity.

The merchant who greets the customer as though the meeting matters, who holds the space with warmth rather than hunger, creates something the transaction itself cannot create: return. Harmony is not a business strategy but a quality of presence that prosperity recognizes and moves toward.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 土 Earth 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese business proverb (common in shop fronts and trade culture)

Read the full reading
347

年nián年nián有yǒu餘yú

nián nián yǒu yú

year after year have surplus

May every year bring abundance and surplus; a traditional blessing for prosperity.

Surplus is not about greed but sufficiency that overflows — the harvest that feeds the table and still has enough to store for the hungry month. The household with余 is a household that can give rather than one that is always calculating whether there is enough. That extra capacity is what makes generosity possible.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional Chinese New Year blessing (very common in festive culture)

Read the full reading
348

臨lín淵yuān羨xiàn魚yú，不bù如rú退tuì而ér結jié網wǎng

lín yuān xiàn yú bù rú tuì ér jié wǎng

standing at the deep pool envying fish, better to step back and weave a net

Wishing for something without preparing is useless; step back and prepare the means.

Longing at the water's edge is not the problem; the problem is calling the longing a plan. The fish will not leap onto the bank out of sympathy for your hunger. The hour spent weaving is the hour that earns you everything the water holds.

諺語 Proverb Wealth, Work & Diligence Present-minded 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Han Shu 漢書·董仲舒傳 (Dǒng Zhòngshū biography)

Read the full reading
349

財cái源yuán廣guǎng進jìn

cái yuán guǎng jìn

may sources of wealth broadly enter

A blessing that wealth may pour in from every direction.

This is the wish pasted by the door in red each new year, and it asks for something specific: not one windfall but many mouths of income, so that if one runs dry the others still run. Abundance is safest when it does not depend on a single source.

俗語 Saying Wealth, Work & Diligence Looks ahead 水 Water 豬 Year of the Pig

Source Traditional auspicious phrase 吉祥話 (spring-festival couplet register); no single classical text — attested folk formula

Read the full reading
350

一yī個gè籬lí笆ba三sān個gè樁zhuāng

yī gè lí ba sān gè zhuāng

one fence three stakes

Even the strongest person needs support; everyone needs others to lean on.

The fence that stands upright does so because it knows which posts to rest its weight against. Pride pretends to need nothing but it is always secretly braced against something. Knowing where your stakes are is honest, and honesty about need is the first gesture of true strength.

俗語 Saying Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 木 Wood 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
351

三sān思sī而ér後hòu行xíng

sān sī ér hòu xíng

think three times, and only then act

Deliberate before you act — turn a decision over more than once before letting it out of your hands. (Confucius wryly noted twice may be enough.)

You already know the choice you make fast and hot is rarely the one you'd sign in the morning. Let the impulse sit overnight; if it still holds at dawn, it was real, and if it doesn't, the pause just quietly saved you.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Analects 論語, Gongye Chang 公冶長

Read the full reading
352

孤gū掌zhǎng難nán鳴míng

gū zhǎng nán míng

one palm hard to clap

One hand cannot clap; nothing can be accomplished alone.

The sound that fills a room requires two surfaces meeting. Even the greatest voice needs a wall to return the echo. Those who insist on doing everything alone often produce half a sound and call it silence's failure rather than their own.

成語 Adage Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Han Fei Zi 韓非子·功名 (Gōng Míng chapter)

Read the full reading
353

話huà不bù投tóu機jī半bàn句jù多duō

huà bù tóu jī bàn jù duō

if talk not resonant, even half a sentence is too many

When there is no rapport, even a half sentence is too much; conversation requires resonance.

Two thousand words with the wrong person leave both parties exhausted and unheard. Half a word with the right one and the whole afternoon is transformed. Conversation is not the transfer of information but the meeting of two minds that recognize something in each other. When that meeting fails, length cannot compensate for the absence.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 水 Water 鼠 Year of the Rat

Source Yuan Dynasty 元·施耐庵《水滸傳》 (Shuǐ Hǔ Zhuàn, Water Margin)

Read the full reading
354

言yán者zhě無wú罪zuì，聞wén者zhě足zú戒jiè

yán zhě wú zuì, wén zhě zú jiè

the one who speaks is without fault; the one who hears takes it as warning

Honest criticism should not be punished, and the listener should take the caution to heart even if it doesn't wholly apply — a rule for keeping counsel open.

When someone risks telling you the hard thing, the part of you that flinches wants to punish them for it. Don't. Thank the messenger, keep whatever fits, and you'll stay the kind of person people are still brave enough to be honest around.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 土 Earth 牛 Year of the Ox

Source Classic of Poetry 詩經, Great Preface 大序

Read the full reading
355

三sān人rén成chéng虎hǔ

sān rén chéng hǔ

three people make a tiger

Repeated rumors, even false ones, come to be believed.

The tiger was never in the marketplace, yet three witnesses convinced a king it was there. Repetition does the work of truth when truth is absent, and the crowd rarely stops to check. What we hear often enough we begin to smell.

成語 Adage Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 木 Wood 虎 Year of the Tiger

Source Warring States 戰國策·魏策二 (Zhàn Guó Cè, Wei Strategies II)

Read the full reading
356

一yī樹shù不bù成chéng林lín

yī shù bù chéng lín

one tree does not make a forest

A single tree cannot make a forest; collective effort is needed for great things.

The single tree is fine and it stands alone beautifully, but it cannot be a forest, and it cannot shelter a village in the way a forest can, or hold the rains, or be a home for the full range of creatures that a forest holds. Some purposes require density, require the gathered plurality. One tree knows this if you listen.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
357

同tóng聲shēng相xiāng應yìng，同tóng氣qì相xiāng求qiú

tóng shēng xiāng yìng tóng qì xiāng qiú

same sound mutually respond, same chi mutually seek

Those of the same nature resonate with each other; like seeks like.

The tuning forks that share a frequency will set each other vibrating across a room without being touched, drawn together only by the air between them. We are also vibrating things, and the ones who make us resonate are often the ones we did not know we had been looking for. The recognition is the resonance.

詩詞 Verse Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 木 Wood 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Yi Jing 易經·乾卦·文言 (Qián Guà, Wén Yán Commentary)

Read the full reading
358

將jiāng心xīn比bǐ心xīn

jiāng xīn bǐ xīn

compare heart to heart

Put yourself in another's shoes; empathize by measuring one heart against another.

The most direct route to understanding another person is to lay your heart beside theirs and see what matches and what differs. This is not agreement but acknowledgment, and the acknowledgment itself changes the conversation. What one knows of one's own loneliness, fear, or hope is the key that opens the door to recognizing those things in someone else's face.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Ming Dynasty 明·馮夢龍《醒世恆言》 (Xǐng Shì Héng Yán)

Read the full reading
359

禮lǐ多duō人rén不bù怪guài

lǐ duō rén bù guài

much politeness, people not blame

Being overly polite never causes offense; courtesy is always appropriate.

The extra bow, the additional pleasantry, the attentiveness that slightly exceeds what was expected — none of these have ever caused a quarrel. Politeness is one of the few things that can be overdone without causing harm, and in an underdone world, the slight excess is often simply received as care. More courtesy is rarely a mistake.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 土 Earth 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (yanyu)

Read the full reading
360

青qīng山shān不bù改gǎi，綠lǜ水shuǐ長cháng來lái

qīng shān bù gǎi lǜ shuǐ cháng lái

green mountain does not change, green water always comes

As long as the green mountains remain, we will meet again; a farewell of hope.

The mountain will still be green when the river rounds back this way again, and this constancy is the promise. Parting is not permanent when the landscape itself has agreed to keep the appointment. We leave with the understanding that the enduring things hold the place until the reunion.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Looks ahead 木 Wood 龍 Year of the Dragon

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying (common farewell and reassurance expression)

Read the full reading
361

病bìng從cóng口kǒu入rù，禍huò從cóng口kǒu出chū

bìng cóng kǒu rù, huò cóng kǒu chū

illness enters through the mouth; disaster comes out of the mouth

Just as sickness comes from what you take in, ruin comes from what you let out — guard your speech the way you guard your food.

The sentence you almost sent, and didn't, saved you more than you'll ever know. Most of your troubles will not come from what was done to you but from something that slipped past your own teeth in a careless second.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 蛇 Year of the Snake

Source Fu Xuan 傅玄, Kou Ming 口銘 (Jin dynasty)

Read the full reading
362

丈zhàng夫fū一yī諾nuò千qiān金jīn

zhàng fū yī nuò qiān jīn

man of standing one promise worth a thousand gold

A person of integrity's single promise is worth a thousand pieces of gold.

The word of the person of character is worth more than the signed document of the person without it, because the signature alone is paper while the character behind it is substance. A promise made from the depths of who one is carries a different weight than one made from the surface of what is convenient in the moment.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 金 Metal 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Shi Ji 史記·季布欒布列傳 (Jì Bù biography)

Read the full reading
363

二èr人rén同tóng心xīn，其qí利lì斷duàn金jīn

èr rén tóng xīn qí lì duàn jīn

two people same heart, their sharpness cuts gold

Two people united in purpose are sharp enough to cut through metal; the power of true partnership.

One edge can slice cleanly, but two edges meeting at the same angle can cut what neither could alone. Partnership is not the addition of two forces but the multiplication of alignment, and the product is something like a blade that the world's resistance cannot blunt. The meeting of two who are genuinely of the same mind is the beginning of almost anything.

詩詞 Verse Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 金 Metal 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Yi Jing 易經·繫辭上 (Xì Cí I, Commentary on Appended Judgments)

Read the full reading
364

人rén多duō力lì量liàng大dà，柴chái多duō火huǒ焰yàn高gāo

rén duō lì liàng dà chái duō huǒ yàn gāo

more people greater strength, more wood higher flame

Strength multiplies with numbers; more people, more power; more contribution, greater result.

The fire that is fed more wood does not merely grow larger; it changes quality at a certain point, becoming capable of warmth and cooking and light that the small flame could not provide. The gathering of contributions is not arithmetic but transformation: at some point of accumulation, the thing becomes something it could not be with less. Add your log.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Traditional Chinese folk proverb (suyu)

Read the full reading
365

同tóng心xīn同tóng德dé

tóng xīn tóng dé

same heart, same virtue

Share the same goals and values; united in heart and purpose.

The team whose hearts point in the same direction and whose standards for good conduct agree with each other is not merely efficient but harmonious, and harmony is the multiplier that effort alone cannot provide. What is truly aligned does not need to be managed; it moves as one thing toward one place.

成語 Adage Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Shang Shu 尚書·泰誓 (Tài Shì, Book of Documents)

Read the full reading
366

志zhì同tóng道dào合hé

zhì tóng dào hé

aspiration same, way aligned

To share the same ideals and pursue the same path; kindred spirits.

There is a particular lightness that comes from walking beside someone who sees the same horizon you do. No translation is needed between people whose purposes already agree. Such companions are not found but recognized, like a key that was always meant for a particular lock.

成語 Adage Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Hou Han Shu 後漢書·劉秀傳 (Liú Xiù biography)

Read the full reading
367

故gù人rén西xī辭cí黃huáng鶴hè樓lóu

gù rén xī cí huáng hè lóu

old friend westward bids farewell at Yellow Crane Tower

The parting of old friends; farewells between the deeply connected.

The Yellow Crane Tower has watched a thousand thousand farewells and not once turned away from watching. The old friend leaves toward the west and the one who watches recedes into the east and between them the river widens into all the distance that will not be crossed for a long time. A farewell between people who have known each other long is a landscape event.

詩詞 Verse Friendship, Trust & Speech Looks back 水 Water 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Li Bai 李白·《黃鶴樓送孟浩然之廣陵》 (Huáng Hè Lóu Sòng Mèng Hàorán Zhī Guǎnglíng, Tang Dynasty poem)

Read the full reading
368

言yán必bì信xìn，行xíng必bì果guǒ

yán bì xìn xíng bì guǒ

speech must be trustworthy, action must bear fruit

Be as good as your word; follow through on what you say.

A promise is a seed and deeds are the water it either receives or does not. The person whose words and actions do not match each other builds nothing that can stand inspection. Trust is the only currency that grows heavier with use.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·子路 (Zǐ Lù XIII)

Read the full reading
369

道dào不bù同tóng，不bù相xiāng為wéi謀móu

dào bù tóng bù xiāng wéi móu

way not same, not mutually planning

Those who follow different principles cannot work together.

Not all incompatibilities are personal; some are structural. Two rivers flowing in opposite directions can be beautiful and vigorous and still have no future together. Knowing when paths are genuinely divergent saves both parties the grief of trying to merge what will not.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 木 Wood 馬 Year of the Horse

Source Analects of Confucius 論語·衛靈公 (Wèi Líng Gōng XV)

Read the full reading
370

一yī花huā獨dú放fàng不bù是shì春chūn，百bǎi花huā齊qí放fàng春chūn滿mǎn園yuán

yī huā dú fàng bù shì chūn bǎi huā qí fàng chūn mǎn yuán

one flower blooming alone is not spring; a hundred flowers blooming together, spring fills the garden

One person's achievement alone is not prosperity; when all flourish together, the whole world blooms.

The single flower is beautiful and entirely itself, but it is not spring. Spring is the condition when a hundred kinds of flowers have decided together that the time has come and the garden fills with the agreement of that collective blooming. What one cannot be alone becomes possible when enough others arrive at the same moment. This is what abundance looks like from inside.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 木 Wood 羊 Year of the Goat

Source Traditional Chinese saying; modern usage in Mao Zedong era

Read the full reading
371

物wù以yǐ類lèi聚jù，人rén以yǐ群qún分fēn

wù yǐ lèi jù rén yǐ qún fēn

things gather by category; people divide into groups

Like attracts like; people naturally form groups with those who share their values and character.

You do not choose your people by accident. They arrive because something in you is already resonating at the same frequency. Pay attention to who keeps appearing in your life — it tells you something true about who you are becoming.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 土 Earth 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source I Ching 易經, Xi Ci Zhuan 繫辭傳 (commentary on the hexagrams)

Read the full reading
372

言yán多duō必bì失shī

yán duō bì shī

many words must lose something

Talk enough and you will inevitably slip — the more you say, the more surface you leave for a mistake to land on.

You have never regretted the thing you didn't say. Watch yourself in the moment you're talking to fill the silence rather than to mean something — that's the exact moment the loose word gets out and takes weeks to walk back.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 金 Metal 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source attested in Zhu Zi Jia Xun 朱子家訓 and earlier; Wiktionary / MOE

Read the full reading
373

近jìn朱zhū者zhě赤chì，近jìn墨mò者zhě黑hēi

jìn zhū zhě chì jìn mò zhě hēi

near red pigment become red, near black ink become black

You are influenced by those you spend time with; environment shapes character.

The cloth dropped into red dye does not resist the color; proximity does the work that intention could not. What surrounds you begins to enter you, the way the fragrance of the room enters the clothing worn in it. Choose your dye carefully, and remember that the people around you are the bath you are sitting in.

諺語 Proverb Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Fu Xian 傅玄·《太子少傅箴》 (Tài Zǐ Shào Fù Zhēn, Admonition to the Prince's Tutor)

Read the full reading
374

過guò河hé拆chāi橋qiáo

guò hé chāi qiáo

cross the river, dismantle the bridge

Once across the river, dismantle the bridge to prevent others crossing; use someone and then abandon them.

The bridge made the crossing possible and the bridge is torn down as the last step of using it. This is the most ungrateful form of completion: to remove what served you in order that it cannot serve others, or perhaps just to prove that you are past it. What crosses safely over gratitude is a person worth knowing; what does not, is not.

成語 Adage Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 木 Wood 猴 Year of the Monkey

Source Yuan Dynasty 元·康進之雜劇 (Yuan Dynasty drama)

Read the full reading
375

心xīn口kǒu如rú一yī

xīn kǒu rú yī

heart and mouth like one

What you say matches what you think and feel; sincerity and consistency between thought and speech.

The person whose words and interior agree with each other does not need to remember what they said, because it was already what they meant. This alignment is not a virtue performed but a state of being that simplifies everything around it. Say what you mean, and mean what you say.

成語 Adage Friendship, Trust & Speech Present-minded 火 Fire 雞 Year of the Rooster

Source Traditional Chinese folk saying

Read the full reading
376

眾zhòng口kǒu鑠shuò金jīn

zhòng kǒu shuò jīn

many mouths melt gold

Public opinion can destroy even the most solid reputation.
